Е*аная х*ета / Unfortunately, very disappointing  I played the demo of this game when it came out and waited for the full release. I found a few minor issues, which I hoped the developers would fix till release, and overall, Constance felt nice and had potential.   Now I played the full game and it’s the most frustrating experience I had with a game in a long while. We’ll start with the good stuff: art style and animation are fine. The platforming is okay. The snapshot mechanic is simple but revolutionary and absolutely genius.  The problem is... everything else.  Fighting system felt strange from the start, since I killed pretty much any enemy faster than it could make even one attack of its own. I thought it was a temporary thing and enemies will get stronger once I get out of the tutorial area. Some of them did… kinda. Every enemy in this game either dies too fast to do anything or is the most infuriating mf you can imagine. And I do not mean difficult to kill, just unbelievably annoying. And their positioning plays specifically into their annoying traits. “This enemy has a big AOE attack? Let’s put it on a platform EXACTLY the size of that AOE. Our enemy is designed to throw hammers and then hide and become invincible? Let’s put it on the ceiling and put only one small platform near that enemy so the player has to jump off every time to deal 1 hit, since the enemy punishes the player for dealing over one hit with a hammer to the face and the platform the player jumped from is going to be the landing spot of all 4 hammers, which renders it very dangerous to return to.”  Paint is a very strange and poorly balanced resource. It’s spent on almost every action except basic attack and movement and you receive damage upon trying to use paint skills with no paint. The result of this ingenious decision? The player gets damaged during platforming sections for using one too many dashes. The player can’t attack or has to take damage to attack a flying boss, because the only vertical attack in the game is a paint skill. The player get’s damaged for moving around the map in the most efficient, fastest way, which is the paint dash. At this point, an inspiration that allows to deal damage to enemies when dashing through instead of dealing it to yourself seems like a necessity instead of a bonus. Nothing but frustration comes out of this limitation the entire game.  Bosses are poorly designed as well. All of them are easy, but I died to almost every one of them at least once because: Firstly, Constance really likes to make the player unable to attack the boss for a certain stage of the fight. It is on the screen, but it’s invulnerable, in an inaccessible position or immediately punishes the player for an attempt to attack too early. Most of the time I couldn’t tell if the game wants me to attack right now or not. Secondly, poorly telegraphed boss attacks. Some attacks are telegraphed very well, others have windup animations that are both short and uninformative. These attacks require memorizing and can barely be avoided without it. Thirdly, health system. The health restores ONLY by breaking vases. Constance does have a self-healing mechanic but it works by storing overheal from those vases. Constance does not get any of this overheal upon respawn so it needs to be farmed before the fight or the fight has to be started without any healing. And finally, the hitboxes. Most of my deaths in this game were due to contact damage. And not even once did I actually see the frame of this contact. Every such death felt like I died of the enemy’s bad breath and not a hit. Which gets us to the respawn mechanic. Upon death the player is given a choice between spawning on checkpoint and perseverance. If “persevere” option is chosen, all enemies get buffed and Constance will now receive damage when trying to dash through them, but enemies drop healing on death. This mechanic, like any other in this game, seems interesting at first and loses any sense the moment you give it some actual thought. Why would I run half of the location from checkpoint if I can just stay here AND get another source of healing, which is usually very limited. Outside of inability to dash through the enemies, the game actually seems farer in this state. Constance also has the worst variation of the wall jump mechanic I’ve ever seen. Instead of being a regular ability to hold onto a wall and jump off it, the player is required to use dash input on the wall which turns Constance into a drop of paint sliding up the wall. This is obviously a PAINt skill and has all the problems related to this fact but it’s also hard to control and sometimes it just refuses to work for no apparent reason. One more frustrating mechanic that could be fixed by simply making it like everyone else. And that can be said about everything else I haven’t listed.  Every mechanic in this game feels like a fun idea that was awfully designed and dramatically undertested. Constance tries to be unique so hard in the things where it is not needed. The result is a huge number of problems which are entirely avoidable and aren’t present in any other metroidvanias I played.  The exploration is linear and boring, not very rewarding either.  The story and dialogue are… existent. And I wish they weren’t. Constance is also full of unnecessarily long and unskippable animations.  My playthrough of this game is the definition of frustration, which is painful to admit since I waited for this game’s release. In the end, it’s a major disappointment that I cannot recommend it to anyone.
Short and sweet game with a beautiful artstyle.

Beautiful little metroidvania with a fun moveset and lovely animations. Fairly short, but that only means that it doesn't overstay its welcome while still providing fun challenges for those that feel up to the task.

Incredibly interesting and creative game. Pretty much every mechanic the game throws at you is interesting, and original. The game is focused around art, and it shows, as even the simple things are done creatively. The game is short, though i believe it's for it's own good, as to not get repetitive. There are some minor flaws, like a runback to a boss being too long, or the lack of fast travel points (though as the map is small, that is not the biggest problem). Otherwise, I enjoyed the game a lot, both in the well made combat, and in the challenging platforming challenges, making you utizilize every gained ability. Difficulty sometimes spikes, but I did enjoy the challenge.  Totally would recommend!

Es un buen juego sin duda. -Los personajes son entrañables y tienen mucha personalidad.  -La temática es única y creo que es una de las cosas que hace brillar a este juego, por su diseño. -En la apuesta jugable han hecho un trabajo magnífico, es divertido de principio a fin y tiene más de un desafío que (sobretodo para completar el juego fuera de su historia principal) te lleva unos "cuantos" intentos. -La historia es interesante porque no trata de ser impredecible si no de transmitir un sentimiento y esto lo hace bastante bien (aunque es alto que solo los más adultos y que trabajaen podrán interpretar).  Mis "peros" -La duración del juego en general es corta, pese a que esto pueda no ser un inconveniente para muchos a mí si me pareció muy corto. -La jugabilidad está muy bien en general, pero tiene un aplique "complejo" que puedes no llegar a aprovechar en una partida normal. Este es mi mayor pero, porque pese a que tiene un enfoque genial y complejo, habrán habilidades principales que se te escapen si no vas a completarlo 100%, además de "modificadores" del combate que le dan una caña adicional de personalización, pero que por su duración no llegarás a aprovechar. En general es un juego genial, pero pudieron exprimirlo un poco más y haber sacado un juego sublime.

Что хотелось бы отметить: 1.Как по мне не очень удобно расставлены спавнпоинты. 2.Некоторые моменты с паркуром основанные на механике прыжков по шипам сделаны довольно душновато. 3.Не всегда интуитивно понятно, где и как выполнять побочные квесты. Платформер неоднозначный. С геймплейной точки зрения в начале всё достаточно просто. И платформинг простой и загадки. Но со временем градус сложности повышается и это идёт игре на пользу. Особенно зубастой игра становится, когда зачищаешь её на 100%. Но тут кроется пару минусов. Без гайда довольно сложно дойти до решения некоторых задач, и есть обязательная для прохождения механика, которой тебя никто не учит, и её можно понять только опытным путём. В остальном: хороший визуальный стиль, приятный эмбиент на фоне, незамысловатая история. По итогу прохождения игра оставила положительное впечатление. Рекомендую к прохождению любителей платформеров.

And to top it all off the platforming in the game is also SO tight that one misinput or incorrect timing punishes you with the a slap against the face. Silksong and even Celeste had movements that are tight but enough room to make some adjustments and here its either you get it frame perfect or bash your head against the wall until you do.  Game was fun until i got more movement and i wish i never did if the game was gonna subject me to such bull!@#$.

Что ж, игра сама по себе неплохая. Классный графон, но здесь слишком много платформинга, прям очень. Большинство боссов проходятся именно на платформенных аренах, и иногда это жостко бесит. Контента тоже слишком мало. Игра буквально на 8 часов прохождения, а если закрывать на 100% - около 15 часов. Главная проблема тут в том что кучу сюжета и контента можно легко пропустить, просто пойдя дальше. Из-за этого конец получается слишком быстрым и рваный. Цена - даже по скидке около 10 долларов за метроидванию, которая проходится за 8 часов, - это чет многовато. Боссы хоть и разнообразные, с хорошими модельками, но у них нету души - чет им не хватает. По сути, если вы любите именно платформеры - вам понравиться. Но если нет, и вам даже в силксонг было сложно прыгать по красным шипам - убегайте от сюда. P.S управление по умолчанию ужас, обязательно меняйте его.

一个中评，这个游戏属于不好也不坏那种，类银小品游戏。 优点：画风音乐不错，地图制作的也还行。 缺点： 1.贵，打折完价格也不便宜，体量很小初玩也就20多个小时就通关。属于空洞等类银游戏的代餐。 2.手感一般，平A和跳跃，冲刺总有卡顿的感觉。尤其是那几个跳跳乐（奶昔挑战）和尖刺陷阱的判定简直逆天，我说你可能直观感受不到，自己玩一下就明白了。下劈是单独按键不是S+攻击键也就导致了跳跳乐总是手忙脚乱按一堆按键。BOSS难度还行也就最后的小丑第三阶段和终极大boss难一点。但boss数量较少，解谜和跳跳乐比较多。尤其是硫酸池那里的推箱子解谜给我干红温了。隐藏墙不看攻略一点也不好找。篝火没有传送功能，只能坐电梯跑图，好在地图不算太大没有跑图跑到吐。 3.剧情有点老套和难绷，经典的因为工作压力大导致的玉米症焦虑症少女拯救自己内心的故事。

Damn. I wanted to like this one. And I really did when I started playing, but the number of times I ended my play session feeling frustrated were just too many. Seeing that it confortably sits at 80%+, I'll just go and write my honest feelilngs about it:  [h3]Good Stuff[/h3] + The game's pretty, and the audio and ambientation overall is very good. + Fluid controls and good game feel overall. + The first "tear flashback" was pretty interesting.   [h3]Not so Good Stuff[/h3] - There are some serious difficulty balancing problems, I think. -- Bosses in general do too much damage. It was impossible for me to beat any of them before dying several times until pretty advanced in the game. Then, when you die, the game lets you decide if you want to instantly retry making the boss more difficult (why in the world would I want that, if I'm still learning how it works, dying in the process???) or respawn on last resting point, making you lose 1 to 3 minutes of your life for no reason. There was a heavy feeling of artificial padding through this dynamic. --> Add to this that a good number of times you learn by being hit, cause there's just no time to react. --> Add to this that the healing capabilities in this game are very limited (until you get enough optional upgrades, at least).  - In general, optional upgrades are... too hidden? Like, I was close to finish the game and I lacked LOTS of "inspirations" (Hollow Knight charms) and slots for them, as well as health and mana upgrades. I lacked lots of everything. This isn't common at all for me in these find of games. I usually backtracked to try and get stuff, but I feel that the way the collectibles are scattered / hidden in this game feels too unnatural. For example, you decide to explore an already visited room, and you just can't find what's left in there (the map says there's something left in the room), or you don't have the ability to get it yet. -> This is, of course, related to boss difficulty. If the upgrades were more "natural" to get, maybe bosses would have been easier. -> Limited fast travel makes this worse.  - There's a chase section (which I like) that was terribly frustrating (which I don't like). Too short time to react to pretty tight platforming / specific mechanic uses, with very short to no margin before having to restart from the beginning. Artificial padding feel again. --> Add to this that the monster that chases you decides to slow down when the level design requires it, making the whole experience even more ridiculous and frustrating when you die moments after, when it arbitrarily just goes ahead and kills you.  - The game just doesn't want to be completed (100%'d). This played a big part in my negative review, as I like to complete games as long as it isn't painful, but oh boy. As said, the game tells you in which rooms you skipped something, which is very appreciated, BUT... -> It happened a lot that I went to an uncompleted room, and I just couldn't find what there was left. No clues whatsoever. Needless to say, this resulted in lots of time lost with void exploration and backtracking. THEN, it turns out that, even though I already beat the last boss and the only thing left to do was try and complete the game, some rooms can only be completed with some random and obscure condition, such as: --> Getting the [spoiler] double jump [/spoiler] ability, which for some reason is optional, and it's so nonsensically hidden, also behind a pretty lazy-designed boss. --> Some rooms feature a platforming challenge (milkshake) that needs to be unlocked at some other specific point of the map (discovered in Youtube, as no clue whatsoever was given). --> Some rooms are gigantic, which makes this worse. --> Some rooms feature a "puzzle" that, depending on the case, can make this much worse (looking at you, room with underwater, move-inside-the-block gameplay and horrible inmortal battery enemies. OH god I hate those).  -> I found a room with an underwater button, that I had no clue how to activate. Mind you, at the endgame, with all the abilities, at the end of a path I'd need to track again in case I wanted to be back there.  -> Some NPCs gave me missions that for the sake of me I had totally no clue about how to complete. When I saw how on Youtube, I was flabbergasted about the happy-idea levels of obscurement.   [h3]Conclusion[/h3] * The game promised a lot, and shone at times, but the sheer frustration during some bosses and sections, and the absolute lack of sense at the time of completing it / try to get some optional stuff added more and more time that felt wasted. And if there's something I can't tolerate in a videogame, is that it does not respect my time.

如果只是要通關，10小時左右就能完成，我100%通關也才花18個小時，但才300多塊的遊戲，內容也不能要求太多。戰鬥方面也沒甚麼亮點，BOSS戰也都簡單，沒有比較讓人驚豔戰鬥，本來不想評論的，但後來被這款的跳跳樂(奶昔挑戰)噁心到了，18個遊戲小時內，完成這個挑戰就不知道花了幾個小時。 很多跳跳樂的關卡都需要很正確的攻擊方向跟手速，用方向鍵會來不及，用搖桿有時候又會攻擊不是自己要的方向，跑關因為這樣常常莫名其妙的失敗；爬牆的手感真的糟糕到不行，遊戲裡面也沒有告知哪些牆不能爬，要玩家自己試才知道；然後最噁心的地方就是電牆的部分，消除的時間超級短，有時候就算動作連貫還會莫名其妙被電到，真心建議消除時間再多給玩家起碼0.3秒。

SO the main attraction of the game is that the asthetics are extremely polished and you can see that it's a beautiful game with some really unique cutscenes and details throughout. Everything on the artistic perspective is really well done.  The gameplay overall is also really solid and enjoyable - especially if you're just going through a casual playthrough I think someone can go through the entire game and really enjoy it. There are only two main points dragging it down. The first is that the bosses can feel a bin unintuitive at times, with some they're really just dodging/waiting games with very little do whilst you're waiting for the bosses to finish their attacks - it feels weird in that they can be right in front of you but still very little room. The other aspect of the bosses is that they can just feel a bit unintuitive - I remember this particularly being the case with a knight and a green boss where I just felt like I wasn't sure what I was supposed to be doing at all - this was also the case with some of the side quests and even main story, with those, they could perhaps put a message in the diary to give a bit of a further hint of where to go.  The other main issue comes with the platforming which varies wildly in difficulty throughout the game, just can be too precise in ways that can feel a bit unfun and at times I felt like (on pc), there was just one too many buttons to press so it became a real feat of finger gymnastics. Mostly this came from different bounciness and hooks which could feel really awkward at times and had kind of un necessary cooldowns which made everything feel really crunchy. Moreover, whilst the respawn mechanic is really nice for dealing with the platforming heavy rooms, the fact that you're abilities end up needing hp after a while to platform ends up in really strange "I need to die because I don't have enough hp to get through this room scenarios).  Overall a really solid and beautifully made game with some kinks surrounding the gameplay which I think could be sorted out but are entirely manageable if frustrating.

Constance's biggest problem is for a lot of people going to be that it's a short game in a genre populated by pretty long games, but if that's not a sticking point for you then it's a pretty good time. The art and animation is beautiful, especially the protagonists, who is imbued with a sense of flowing, liquid movement that reminds of Splatoon of all things. I found the difficulty curve kind of uneven, with boss fights requiring far more precision than the moment-to-moment platforming, though there's a fair share of optional platforming challenges that were delightfully, fiendishly hard, especially in the endgame. The game's story of depression and artistic burnout didn't really land the way I was hoping it would, it felt pretty rote and predictable to me, but there are some moments where it tries to express those feelings mechanically, and those moments actually worked really well.  Overall I really did enjoy my time with Constance, and I do not regret paying full price for it despite its brevity. Also the final boss rips.

El mensaje y la narrativa también. Tiene detalles chidos como la forma de acomodar tus habilidades y el mejorarlas. Ni con la última actualización se salva el mapa: su visualización es horrible y confunsa (pero el detalle de las fotografías como puntos de referencia está increíble). TOTALMENTE RECOMENDADO.

Constance is, overall, an excellent metroidvania, carried by a lovely aesthetic, buttery (or perhaps painterly?) smooth controls, and competent level design. The caveats are its relatively short length (I'm honestly surprised I spent almost 7 hours on it; it felt much shorter, and I suspect a not-insignificant amount of that time was spent alt-tabbed), and storytelling that feels clunky and disconnected, despite the consistently charming art and character design that accompanies it.   I'd say check it out if you love metroidvanias and don't mind a middling narrative or short length.

Constance ist ein wunderschönes Spiel und ich kann es jeden empfehlen, der nach Hollow Knight nach dem nächsten tollen Metroidvania sucht. Besonders lobenswert ist der Art Style und die spaßigen und toll-designten Bosse. Für viele könnte das Spiel zu kurz sein, da ich aber eine Person bin, der gerne viele unterschiedliche Spiele spielt, war die Spiellänge für mich perfekt. Das Combat fühlt sich sehr smooth an, zwar nicht so perfekt wie ein HK und Nine Sols, aber auf jeden Fall sehr spaßig. Das Spiel ist im Vergleich zu anderen Spiele im Genre sehr einfach, was aber für Einsteiger zu Gute kommt. Die Story ist sehr schön, mit der ich sehr relaten kann, von der ich aber gerne mehr gesehen hätte. Damit verbunden, dass das Spiel kein Secret Ending hat, was bei solchen Games eigentlich üblich ist, ist das Ende anfangs erdrückend, jedoch später sehr realitätsnah und zufriedenstellend. Mein einziger Kritikpunkt wäre, dass die Nebenmissionen leider nicht so schön gestaltet waren. Teilweise sind manche Missionen nicht ganz selbsterklärend oder sind an bestimmten Punkte von anderen Nebenmissionen abhängig. Abgerundet ist das Spiel sehr empfehlenswert und ich wünsche dem Studio viel Erfolg bei ihren nächsten Projekten. Super Job :)

Первый час игры ощущается как что-то неплохое с зачатком удачных решений для исследования мира. По итогу его исследовать не хочется, платформинг без права на ошибку и с полным заучиванием маршрута,. Боевка без вау эффекта(удар ударом и благо противник сдох). Только визуал в относительном плюсе, сюжет упоминания не стоит

Если проходить только "сюжет", не обращать внимания на побочные квесты, не пытаться исследовать всё на 100% и взять игру со скидкой ~80%, то это неплохая игра на 6-8 часов.  Однако, если "проходить" всё и купить игру за фулл прайс - лучше пройти мимо этой игры. Платформиг - поверхностный, а в моментах с "секретками" усложнён ради усложнения. Сюжетный "момент" с побегом от пасти и сбор коктейлей ради ачивки это отдельный вид мазохизма.  Видов врагов мало, боссы скучные и боевая система в целом слишком простая и безыдейная.  Количество "скамеек" для отдыха очень мало, а мест телепорта откровенно недостаточно.  Карта и миникарта сделаны хорошо, но то, что карта отображает только наличие "лавок и точек интереса" на локации, а не их расположение  - откровено непонятное решение.   Даже не стоит называть какие игры были "реферансами" для разработчиков, а вот то, что идеи механик взяты бездумно и без понимания почему в других играх они хороши, а тут нет - это печально.  Итог: стоит брать только по очень большой скидке и только если других не пройденных игр такого жанра уже не осталось.

Honnêtement, j'ai du mal à savoir si je recommande ce jeu... c'est vraiment propre à chacun-e dans ce cas de figure...  La qualité du travail est présente, c'est indéniable (et étudiant le game design et le word building je vois bien le boulot derrière...malgré tout je ne peux pas aller contre mon propre avis et ne vous citer que le positif)... aujourd'hui je vais devoir donner mon avis de Joueuse. Donc je ne vais pas recommander en notation, mais vous dire ce que j'ai apprécié et moins aimé.  Déjà, je rebondis sur le commentaire de Python nous disant que pour un jeu qui traite de la santé mentale, on aura vite des problème avec notre santé mentale en y jouant...et je ne peux qu'adhérer.   Oui, le jeu est sublime, les jeux de mots sont sympathiques, les animations d'une fluidité absolue. Ensuite, pour les ennemis, ça va dépendre des goûts de chacun-e. Une difficulté complètement aléatoire je trouve. Le fait de devoir retourner dans des zones pour débloquer des passages est ok, et même normal, mais également complètement (trop) dispersé et aléatoire, mais le fait que notre trajet (et donc les visuels des salles traversées) disparaisse (comme un gommage, on a compris toutes les références graphiques évidemment), peut s'avérer assez terrible côté mémoire.  Pour ce qui est du sujet, je trouve le sujet de cette jeune femme/jeune fille bien traité (ayant moi-même été en dépression et dans une relation dont j'ai du m'échapper pour ma santé mentale, et étant graphiste également), cependant, il faut vraiment avoir 'la récompense' du Lore, pour 'sauver' mentalement notre protagoniste. Et les rares scènes qu'on nous offre en récompenses de passages prenant des heures ne sont clairement pas assez longues ou, encore une fois, pas bien équilibrées face au trop plein qu'on s'est tapé (deux boss et un plateforming avec course poursuite ; 5 boss juste pour une zone, pas d'autre chose forcément 'amusante' à côté, etc.).  Je viens de passer un temps fou au cirque, pour des boss à la fois top pour le gameplay, mais redondants à souhaits...et pour les joueurs-euses d'Hollow Knight je vais le dire : J'avais l'impression de me taper un mélange entre le Panthéon et le Colisée des Fous ! Pour une partie sensée 'détendre' mais surtout PAS de façon 'annexe', comme un Colisée forcé... parfois on se demande s'il nous manque des power-ups alors que pas du tout.  Beaucoup de surstimulation pour 'pas grand chose' (je ne parle pas du jeu mais du résultat obtenu après avoir triomphé).  Maintenant, le gameplay est bien amené, le game design également, le système des autels est hyper agréable, le level design...à rééquilibrer, les musiques...ça dépend lesquelles, quand on est longtemps dans une zone personnellement je ne peux plus les entendre...alors qu'au début je les aimais bien.  Je ne laisse jamais de commentaire si long, mais je suis mitigée, vous l'aurez compris, j'adore ce jeu autant que je le trouve très mal équilibré en somme ! On ne lâche rien surtout pour avoir la suite de l'histoire...et encore.  En revanche...oui je le conseillerais aux personnes qui ont besoin 'de leur bulle' mine de rien. Attendez-vous simplement à devoir faire preuve de beaucoup, BEAUCOUP de patience. Et mettez un pomodoro ou timer pour avoir une vie à côté et prendre des pauses...

Lo que más me ha gustado de Constance es, sin duda, el diseño artístico y cómo se sienten las mecánicas. El juego entra muchísimo por los ojos: cada zona está súper cuidada, con un estilo de dibujo muy marcado que me recuerda bastante a Hollow Knight, pero con personalidad propia. Los fondos tienen mil detalles que le dan ambiente a cada área y hacen que todo se sienta vivo. La zona del circo es la que más me ha divertido, tanto por los colores como por lo amplia que es y la cantidad de secretos que tiene, especialmente entre las nubes. También me ha encantado la zona más “mágica”, que me recordó a una zona del Elden Ring por la temática y la decoración. A nivel jugable, lo mejor es lo fluido que se mueve el personaje: todo responde al instante y puedes encadenar movimientos como si fuera un baile. Mecánicas como meterte en paredes o interactuar con las bolas blancas del entorno, me parece lo más diferente de las mecánicas que he visto en otros juegos de este género. El plataformeo me ha parecido muy bien ajustado, desafiante pero justo, y me ha gustado mucho el sistema de mejoras tipo “Tetris” porque te obliga a pensar cómo encajar de la mejor manera las habilidades.  Lo que menos me ha gustado es que algunas zonas, como la más tipo cloaca y la amarilla del norte, se me han hecho menos interesantes visualmente. En combate, aunque los bosses y minibosses están bien diseñados y son divertidos, el boss final se me ha quedado corto: me esperaba algo más complejo, con más fases y combinaciones que realmente te pusieran a prueba. También he echado en falta más dificultad en algunos combates. Y otro punto flojo son los NPCs, que se sienten bastante planos porque sus diálogos no cambian a medida que avanzas en la historia, lo que hace que el mundo pierda un poco de vida.

To the point ===================== A decent Metroidvania that probably should have stuck to being a platformer, this game tells a bit of a pretentious story way worse then some similar games. I'd skip it unless it's on sale.  Good Stuff ================ - The Bosses in this game, every single one, in fact, are extremely fun. Challenging but with patterns that you can pick up with a little observation, I really did enjoy every single one from start to finish. Would love a boss rush mode or something similar.  - The movement tech in this game is a lot of fun and quite unique as you turn into a blob of paint to slide up walls and slip through spikes. I greatly enjoyed hopping from room to room or grappling off enemies, I just wish it wasn't tied to your current paint mechanic as it tends to run out fast and once it does you need to burn health until it regenerates.  - The sprite work in this game, especially for the characters, is very well done and quite eye catching, contrasting with what I would consider to be very dull backgrounds. I enjoyed how the bosses even use some mixed media ( or a simulation) in their designs.  Bad Stuff ================  - This game so desperatley wants to be Celeste, but where that game had a far more subtle way of delivering its message via it's story, this game is incredibly ham-fisted about it. It deals with serious topics but rams them down your throat perhaps out of a fear of being too vague. The story comes off as a boring, by the numbers mental health tale that doesn't feel rewarding to finish. To top it all off the game ends SO abruptly. I was expecting more and then poof, it's done and I felt a bit slighted.  - While the movement tech is a ton of fun, combat rather bland by comparison. I had tons more fun flying around from room to room then stuck in tedious combat after combat since everything boils down to the same few moves on repeat from the first boss to the last.  - You have to do a few stupid art minigames and god are they slow. From filling a scrapbook with correct pictures to painting a picture where you have to go back to a pallete over and over again since you cna only use a color once, the only saving grace is that they're infrequent.  - The world seems so dull and lifeless and all the zones are painfully generic. While the art is really something, the zones really feel like they have very little depth and breadth.  - I feel like you are sort of frontloaded with movement powers, I got a few in rapid succession which never really gave me time to play around with one for long and the smaller map really diminishes how much time you have to rely on them.  - The map interface is weird, for some reason it's always full zoomed out by default and you have to zoom in to your zone. Also no mini-map or anything from what I could see.  - The game seems so rushed, like there was meant to be more but it was cut for time. I clocked in at 4 hours or so with 65% ish completed. I'm sure there's more to see but there's no alternate endings and I only missed a single boss apparently.

[h1]個人的に好みではないが、よく練られた良作メトロイドヴァニア[/h1]  「筆」で戦うスタイルが特徴的なメトロイドヴァニアです。 システム面ではこのジャンルの王道を行く作りになっており、特筆して奇抜な要素はないものの、同ジャンルのファンであれば安定して楽しめるクオリティに仕上がっています。  [h2]特徴と気になった点[/h2]  [b]・アスレチックと弾幕回避がメイン[/b] 本作は直接的な戦闘よりも、アスレチック要素や弾幕を避けるアクションに比重が置かれています。これは道中だけでなくボス戦でも顕著です。  [b]・難易度とストレス要素[/b] ボス自体の強さは、他のいわゆる「死にゲー」系メトロイドヴァニアと比較すると控えめです。 ただ、リトライ性の悪さや、後半「アスレチックをこなした後にボス戦が始まる」という構成が増えるため、この点は人によってストレスに感じるかもしれません（私個人としては少し合いませんでした）。  [h2]総評[/h2]  UIは非常に親切に設計されており、遊びやすさへの配慮が感じられます。 ボス戦のギミックも、こういったアクション性が好きな人には刺さるはずです。  個人的な好みからは外れましたが、ゲームとしての完成度は高いため「オススメ」とさせていただきます。

LO MEJOR - Es muy bonito visualmente - Es divertido y entretenido - Está muy bien optimizado, no he encontrado bugs graves y puede funcionar en ordenadores que no sean muy potentes   LO PEOR - Poca variedad de enemigos - En algunos momentos, tiene picos de dificultad mas altos que pueden llegar a ser un poco frustrantes.    -OPINIÓN PERSONAL-  La historia es sencilla pero reflexiva, la cual queda muy bien con como está diseñado el juego en base a la jugabilidad y gráficamente.   El juego, visualmente es bonito, llamativo y colorido, no tiene gráficos ultra realistas, sino mas bien un estilo de dibujos animados, el cual como digo, le queda genial al juego y además, eso hará que se pueda jugar en ordenadores que no sean muy potentes. Eso sí, hay en algunas partes que tiene picos de dificultad que pueden hacer que te frustres, pero igual que la historia de este juego, puedes parar a descansar cuando estés frustrado/saturado, volver a intentarlo de nuevo para que veas que eres capaz de lograrlo.

The game is absolutely wonderful. the atmosphere is stunning, the art style is gorgeous, and the movement feels great. The story is solid, the world is beautiful, and I truly had a blast playing it.  I’ll compare it to its closest peer, Hollow Knight. From my perspective, I actually enjoyed Constance more, but Hollow Knight is superior in several areas. First, the bosses here are too easy. so much so that I don’t even remember a single one of them, despite playing through the game multiple times to hit 100% completion. No boss really stuck with me.  The game is also very short and the world feels small. I wished for a larger world and more challenging boss fights. In these two specific aspects, Hollow Knight is a level above Constance.  Constance feels like an 'incomplete project' with massive potential, but it lacks a vital factor for a Metroidvania in my opinion: the challenge of bosses and the sense of getting lost in the world.   This doesn’t mean I didn’t enjoy Constance in fact, I enjoyed it more than Hollow Knight. I even believe it has the potential to rival Silksong, but it’s currently a long way off. If I were to give Silksong a 10, Constance would get a 4, and Hollow Knight would be a 3.8 ( This rating compared to Silksong ).

Great gameplay, Great visuals 8/10

Overall, playing Constance was an enjoyable experience. I think it's obvious that the game is heavily inspired by Hollow Knight, which is fine by me. Graphics are pretty. Music is decent without being memorable at any point. The story is fine. However, it is presented in a way that sometimes literally takes you out of the game. Apart from that, it lacks the typical world lore depth that is usually conveyed through items, map, discovery, etc in other games like this. Controls are fine for the most part. Some of the platforming challenges were a bit annoying, especially teleports placed too close to each other. My biggest issues with the game were basically two: difficulty and enemies. The game is simply too easy. I don't need it to be nightmarishly difficult, but in Constance most bosses took one or two tries to beat. Regarding enemies, I thought there was very little variety and, more importantly, very few bosses. I still enjoyed the game and it took about 20 hours to 100% my run without external help.

I'm gonna be honest. I'm sad that I can't refund this anymore.  I really wanted to give this game a fair try but I can't anymore  My biggest issue with this game is that you are punished for needing to avoid too much. In a game like this where you have many tricky attacks from enemies and bosses, the one thing you never want to do IS PUNISH THE PLAYER FOR NOT BEING GOOD AT NATURALY AVOIDING ATTACKS  This isn't the only issue with the game. The revive mechanic is also a mess. You choose to respawn in the room you died in but now you can't dash through enemies. Just don't let me revive then. So many enemies/bosses clearly aren't designed or placed in adequate spots to reasonably avoid them WITHOUT dashing through them.  The "Slam"/pogo is also just not fun to use with controller. Half the time I miss a target after being confident that I would hit it and then fall into the void, an attack or enemy. Locking movement to perform a pogo is not a fun because it's very easy to completely miss the target and then get hit for no reason other than being slightly off  The final nail was the [spoiler] Circus. It's just not fun. It expects you to constantly use your abilities and dashes but again YOU GET PUNISHED FOR USING THEM TOO MUCH. There's an inspiration you can equip to make it so the dashes don't damage you BUT YOU NEED TO UPGRADE YOUR ART BOOK SO YOU CANT EVEN EQUIP IT WHEN YOU GET IT. I understand that there's probably an upgrade I missed but I shouldn't even be taking damage simply because I'm trying to AVOID taking damage, that makes literally no sense [/spoiler]  To be honest, it just feels like I'm getting punished for "Being too much" (based on the theme of the game) which isn't an enjoyable feeling because I did nothing wrong. If I use my abilities too much, if I try to attack too much, if I avoid too much. All these things cause me to get punished and it's BS.   I just want to play and enjoy the story and graphics of a game, I didn't buy this to suffer just because the dev decided to punish people for doing too much. That's just BS

Это просто ахуенно. Как по мне, незаслуженно недооценённая игра. Ну и жаль что так мало. Прохождение займет в районе 6-8 часов, на 100% 10-12 (для сейва, не ачивок).  Говорят что в этой игре затрагивают глубокие социальные темы, выгорание, усталость от работы, важность отдыха и т.п. И да, всю игру насквозь пронизывает данная тема, но я конкретно с таким не сталкивался, ну или просто тугой, так что не могу оценить данную часть.   Так что перейдем к геймплею. Визуал приятный, саунд хороший и подходящий под ситуации. Детальные и красивые анимации. Прикольная реализация механики возрождения, особенно помогает при прохождении паркура. Босы и мобы интересные, и в плане дизайна и в плане атак. Но вот сами боевки средние, все просто сводится к спаму "лкм". Минимапа, тут не все так однозначно. Идея показывания маршрута и реализация просмотра карты (tab) топ, возможность в лейте узнать где ты что пропустил топ, визуально тоже топ, но вот понимание, что где и почему, ужасное (но мб я тугой). Есть небольшие проблемы с экономикой, особенно если вы не пропускаете торгашей и находите их всех сразу.  Если так почитать, то даже сразу нельзя понять почему игра так хороша, минусов довольно много, так в чем же подвох? Вспоминаем основную цель игры, поднять тему выгорания и усталости. Она буквально хочет что бы ты играл в нее спокойно.* Из-за этого довольно простая боевка не кажется огромным минусом, проблемы с экономикой так же игнорируются. Да иногда она может дать тебе сложный бой или паркур но оно не ощущается чем-то ужасным.   В общем хороший платформер с довольно глубоким смыслом и приятным. Любителям холыча думаю зайдет, новичкам тоже.   *Не хотел это впихивать в текст так что просто сделал выноску. ДАЛЬШЕ МОГУТ БЫТЬ СПОЙЛЕРЫ.  Учитывая то что главный злодей это буквально негативная часть гг, которая использует нити для всяких приколов (надеюся я правильно это понял). Теперь вспоминаем что при смерти мы можем "встать" в той же комнате где и умерли, но этим усилим врагов, и при этом у нас в углах экрана появляются те самые нити как и у злодея. Игра буквально говорит тебе пойди отдохни, не переусердствуй. Но это так, мои мысли.

[h1]Spiel[/h1] Willkommen zu meinem Constance Review Abenteurer! Dieses Metroidvania Spiel bietet wieder eine kleine Story, eine bunte Welt mit verschiedenen Charakteren. Das Gameplay gestaltet sich als eines der schweren in dieser Kategorie. Ihr spielt Constance, eine Künstlerin dessen Waffe ein Pinsel ist. Ja... ein Pinsel. Also pinselt ihr eure Gegner im wahrsten Sinne des Wortes ins Gesicht. Oder wo anders hin. Im Laufe des Spiels könnt ihr viele passive Fähigkeiten freischalten, die im Laufe des Spiels benötigt werden. Eine tolle Funktion ist die Kamera. Mag komisch klingen, aber mit dieser könnt ihr am jeweiligen Standort ein Foto schießen. Dieses sieht man auf der Karte. Damit kann man sich stellen markieren, an denen man noch etwas zu erledigen hat.  [h1]Schwierigkeitsgrad[/h1] Die Schwierigkeit des Spiels empfand ich stellenweise schwer. Boss Kämpfe haben ihre Mechaniken. Lernen und dementsprechend handeln. Die kleineren, normalen Gegner in der Welt sind angenehm zu besiegen. Solltet ihr einmal in eine Stachel oder aus der Welt fallen, sterbt ihr nicht sondern verliert nur einen kleinen Teil eures Lebens und werden zum Standpunkt des Falls zurück teleportiert. Durch finden von Stücken könnt ihr euere Lebenspunkte erweitern. Ebenso durch mögliche Käufe mit der gefundenen Währung von Gegnern. Solltet ihr einmal verlieren, könnt ihr euch an der Stelle wiederbeleben. Allerdings werden Gegner/Bossen vorübergehend stärker bis ihr beschließt euch am Speicherpunkt wieder zu beleben. Leider ist kein Schwierigkeitsgrad aus wählbar.  [h1]Spielzeit[/h1] Story: Nach 3,5 Stunden hatte ich 48% des Spiels abgeschlossen. Vermutlich spielt man es ca. 7 Stunden? Natürlich je nachdem wie gut/schnell ihr seid. Da mir das Spiel zu stressig wurde, habe ich es beendet.  [h1]Spiele-Leistung&Grafik[/h1] Grafikeinstellungen: Gibt es keine  Auflösung: 4k (3840*2160) FPS: Problemlos mit 120 FPS spielbar. GPU Auslastung lag bei ~23 bis 30%.  [h1]Fazit[/h1] 19,50€ scheinen für das Spiel gut zu sein. Sofern man schwere Metroidvania Spiele mag. Meins war es leider nicht.

I just want to say this is a wonderful accidental find, just combing through some lists of metroidvanias that I haven't seen. I love hand painted, vanilla ware remains some of the most artistically beautiful gaming anytime, but this is coming darn close. Everything about the aesthetics to the mystery behind this young heroine is gripping. The platforming is moderate difficulty comma and I am completely fine with that. A lot of games now under the heading of precision platformer are a slog. There's nothing wrong with a mild challenge and delicious exploration. I have not played long enough to know if the later chapters increase the difficulty. The boss fights are wonderfully retro, and you can learn patterns and movements with relative ease. I'm really very impressed and I highly recommend this game. Emotionally, it may tell a challenging story behind the scenes.But it does so in a way that leaves me comfortable and reflective.  I have no quibbles with the mechanics or the overall background programming. There have been no game crashing bugs,  no way to put the game in an unwinnable state. The love and care that this development team showed this product is aspirational, and I truly wish more games had this kind of attention to detail.And perfection.  My one criticism would be the initial adjustment to the jumping and the ability to carry out secondary button presses or movements while doing that. Before you acquire some of the paint related actions comma the jumping is a little janky. Even then, it's not really clear to me the power differential between the paint strike and the regular attack button. I found the ability to acquire lifestones and glimmer laughably easy, but in the first five hours or so I haven't had a lot to spend it on, and I am wondering where this goes.   Edit: okay I played long enough to figure out what was bothering me. The fact that she cannot swing her paint brush overhead with the normal attack is not a cool limitation. Furthermore, the fact that I can't make her turn into paint on any direction except horizontal, also is very limiting. I like the grappling hook enough, but I still think you could have done much more with the mechanics in a much more elegant way.  In an age where increasing precision and timing is sort of the holy grail of a lot of new platformers, this is a beautiful and calm game that stays true to the roots of what I would consider a metroidvania.  I give it nine out of ten, the loss of a point really due to the operations and mechanics until the game gets further advanced. I suspect there will be patches that make the jumping smoother, and I look forward to that. Thank you so much for making such a beautiful game.

Minha opinião sobre a historia: A historia desse jogo é algo visto nos dias atuais, ele mostra como a vida realmente é, ela historia te prende cada vez mais no jogo, apesar de ter se inspirado em outros jogos, ele possui sua própria individualidade, e certamente recomendo para qualquer pessoa jogar esse jogo.  Gameplay: Não tive problemas com a gameplay em nenhum momento, o jogo é muito bem feito, e quase não notei nenhum bug, pode jogar sem medo.  Trilha sonora:10/10, A trilha sonora desse jogo é perfeitamente linda, principalmente a ultima musica, do boss final.  data de zeramento: 23/03/2026  Sobre o jogo:Constance é um jogo metroidvania 2D focado em narrativa, desenvolvido pela Blue Backpack e publicado pela BTF GMBH. A história acompanha uma artista lutando contra seus demônios internos (burnout, depressão e ansiedade) em um mundo de tinta em decadência, apresentando visuais desenhados à mão, combate técnico com um pincel e exploração não linear

(重新思考了一会，还是改成好评，鼓励下独立游戏制作组吧) 不推荐，完全不推荐，我能感受到制作组的心意，也知道他们很努力的想做好它，但这款游戏真的非常不好玩 ​常用键有六个，但我只有五根手指，逆天 ​boss战全是屎，我没有开玩笑，全是屎，90%是对面超雄连击打完一套，休息一会继续超雄连击，你只能看表演，逆天 最终boss倒是没有超雄连击，大多数的流程很不错，但中途逼着你跳跳乐，时效内不通关就把你打大残，还tm不止一次，再加上六个常用键，太牛了，把我打红温不止一次，逆天 ​地图探索很难，真的很难，你要知道我是连蔚蓝都通关的人，能说出“很难”代表着什么不言而喻，逆天 ​剧情有两大逆天点，第一是主角的设定，你用同事/朋友视角来看她是一个有事不说找她不回事情不做的奇女子，我知道她很努力了但路人眼里的她真的是这样，第二是omori的既视感有点太严重了 ​(我知道，我知道，“心理疾病”，但你最起码给个理由啊，我除了“主角康斯坦丝，是一个艺术家，好像有个哥哥”以外什么一点都不了解她，再加上用第三人称看康斯坦丝这个人真的一言难尽，我又怎么共情) ​美工很棒，墨水的设定很棒，其他的，一言难尽 ​以及，我12个小时就通关了，但你还是要了我80块，30块顶天了，剩下的钱能不能退我 建议如下 第一，巨大史莱姆boss整个重做，打这玩意纯粹是折磨，马戏团长多加点不同的攻击，最终boss的跳跳乐失败不扣血或者干脆删了 第二，优化跳跳乐 第三，增加更多康斯坦丝的剧情，让我们知道她是什么人，其他类似游戏的主角剧情虽然也不多，但他们是正面角色，没人会讨厌这种人，而本作中我们虽然知道康斯坦丝很痛苦也带给别人痛苦，但剧情还是太少了，给人的观感真的很糟，请加入更多优化后的剧情 我很喜欢这游戏的设定，也很喜欢“墨水”为主题的战斗方式，但其他地方真的一言难尽，希望改的更好

Has potential, but it also has some fluidity errors and input lags randomly and it happens too much in some crucial moments that it just tires you out, what I'm saying is that for how much at some point the game want you to be precise it doesn't give you enough elements for succeeding out of pure skill, above all the air movements feels way more wrong in fluidity than many other games, being dash or simple movement. Well anyway I'm tired of putting up with the lacks of games and compensate for it when the games demands from the player what they don't provide it in a truly reliable way. A shame, since the art style was wonderful and it seemed interesting. Edit: I finished the game at 100% but honestly it's a bit random that you have to defeat a specific boss after you get multiple paths very accessible without going in a specific order because else you can't confront other bosses very well without it dropping an essential power up [spoiler](Dark Embrace). Yes you get it from the first boss you choose after the multiple paths are accessible, but honestly there is only one boss that is bearable without it with the game being imprecise in crucial situations.[/spoiler] Anyway after that a lot of shortcomings I've said are not exactly gone but way more bearable, still new shortcomings arrive after a while, namely the pogo jump is very unreliable in many situations where it needs to be used with precision, above all when you need to cancel it with a dive it's a bit random if you succeed or not, and the other shortcoming is that [spoiler]the Lord Koba bossfight cube sections feel very imprecise and sometimes you're stuck in a forced loop when it attacks with the black spheres until the cube resets, I mean why even give the chance if you can go around endlessly without succeeding in attacking the boss, at that point it's just better to be hit and reset without any chance.[/spoiler] Anyway the enemies were interesting, the platforming challenges and enigmas were interesting, a shame that for some the precision problems of the games shows too much, and the bosses where sometimes incredible sometimes good and sometimes so so, I guess it's a mixed bag [spoiler](above all I don't get why those are the encore versions of the carnival bosses, they were way easier than their base version for me)[/spoiler], and I can confirm even more that the art style is awesome

Было правильным решением чуть подождать - и баги пофиксили, и карту улучшили. Исследование локаций очень понравилось, особенно гениальна система скриншотов интересных мест прямо в игре - допустим, видишь ты место, в которое пока не можешь попасть, делаешь скриншот, и он сохраняется меткой на карте. Потом знаешь, когда туда вернуться.  К слову, я вовремя задумался, пока проходил: "А есть ли тут двойной прыжок?" Потому что некоторые места ну прямо кричали, что он должен быть. Не удержался, загуглил. Оказалось, что есть. Но его было легко пропустить. И я как раз был на том этапе, где его можно было найти. Мне повезло, но вот некоторые, судя по отзывам, даже игру прошли без него. Довольно неприятная мелочь была бы, но тут фортануло мне.  Несмотря на то, что это больше платформер, чем экшн, некоторые боссы вызывали у меня жопоболь. Но возвращаться к ним быстро, и учишься тоже недолго. Самым сложным был слизняк на полэкрана, который постоянно спамил врагами и кислотой с потолка. Иногда это превращалось в бой с рандомом - повезет с каплями с потолка или нет. К слову, его усложнили именно в последнем патче: до этого, судя по прохождениям на ютубе, капель с потолка не было. Вообще боссы интересные, со своими механиками и разнообразием. Мне понравилось. Но всё равно иногда кричал в экран.  Играется приятно, отклик идеальный, управление тоже - всё плавно и гладенько, никаких инпут лагов. Рисовка радует глаз. Визуально с игрой всё очень хорошо.  Основной минус, который могу выделить - скудная система быстрого перемещения. Пять лифтов на всю карту, и от каждого лифта приходилось бегать пешком. Если проходить только сюжет, то это норма, но ведь побочки гоняют по разным местам...  К слову о побочках - пару штук приходилось проходить с подсказками. Особенно квест с телефонными линиями - они никак на карте не отображаются, а бродить по всей карте в поисках - ну такое себе.  А, ещё молочные коктейли. Вот уж что посложнее боссов было. С них у меня знатно пригорало. Были действительно сложные.  А вот за пометку о зачистке каждого сегмента карты жирнющий плюс. Было с кайфом зачищать каждый кусочек. Правда эту пометку я не сразу понял, наверное только на 2/3 игры осознал, что штриховка значит полную зачистку. А, ну и вызываемая мини-карта в игре практически бесполезная. Было удобнее открывать на полный экран.  Игра короткая, я бы даже ещё сыграл. 9.5/10.
It's a good game. Beautiful, many interesting enemies, nice puzzles, creative mechanics, a map big enough. But not really a challenge when it comes to boss fight.

Apesar de curto, Constance conseguiu ser uma experiência maravilhosa de se jogar. Mesmo sendo um metroidvania, gênero que já possui muitos títulos no mercado, o jogo traz temas sérios da realidade, como esgotamento mental e a busca pela perfeição artística, que podemos interpretar como a tentativa incessante de ser perfeito e agradar os outros a todo custo. Tudo isso é retratado conforme você avança na história. São assuntos que fazem você refletir sobre acontecimentos que já passaram na sua vida — ou que ainda podem acontecer — e buscar formas de se melhorar como pessoa, embora isso nem sempre seja fácil, sendo um caminho, na maioria das vezes, árduo.  Falando agora da gameplay, a movimentação é extremamente fluida, com uma arte muito charmosa que combina com cada aspecto do cenário. Já o combate também é fluido; porém, na minha opinião, poderia ter um refinamento melhor, pois certas habilidades do jogo são meio inconsistentes em relação ao que se propõem a fazer.  Sobre os bosses, todos têm designs ok e padrões de ataque um pouco repetitivos, o que acaba tornando as lutas não muito dinâmicas, se resumindo a bater, esperar e bater novamente até a derrota do chefe. Mesmo assim, cumprem o seu papel.  Agora, sobre a exploração e os inimigos, infelizmente esse é o pior ponto do jogo. O level design é bastante repetitivo e não faz um bom uso das novas habilidades adquiridas ao longo da jornada, dando a sensação de que havia boas ideias porém não foram bem executadas e aproveitadas. Já os inimigos são extremamente repetitivos e não conseguem ser carismáticos de forma alguma, tornando-se completamente esquecíveis com o tempo.  Quanto às músicas, elas são incríveis porém, poderiam ser melhores. Ainda assim, é possível apreciá-las.  No geral, Constance se mostra uma experiência única e marcante, tanto pelos temas abordados quanto pelo universo e história apresentados. Entretanto, falha em aspectos primordiais do seu gênero, como um bom level design, bons inimigos e áreas interessantes para se explorar.  Nota final: 6.5/10  Uma obra de arte quase perfeita!!

本作在同类型游戏里比较友好，死后可以原图复活，复活所带来的惩罚机制也没有很大的影响，美术我也很喜欢，但是游戏整体很一般或者说差劲，首先游戏流程极短，难度方面个人而言过低，除了后期的几个奶昔挑战以外，其余的跳跳乐像是在过家家，部分boss机制挺有趣的，其余很多都像来凑数的，很难给人留下印象，游戏的地图设计也很差，单一地图非常空旷，让人没有任何探索的欲望，游戏里大量的法术技能根本是可有可无的，只是让我一周目后做全成就时多几个探图点，货币资源也是，后期大量溢出的点数除了完成部分成就以外根本没有任何用处，这点也极大减弱了我所说的对于地图探索的积极性，哪怕多个功能，花钱给人物模型染个色什么的都能给到玩家一定的情绪价值并且也贴合游戏“画&画笔”的主题。最后一点让我想吐槽的是，这类游戏，剧情故事以及中间的过场动画都是我很期待的点，本作的过场动画以及过场的小游戏我很喜欢，但是剧情呢，结尾主角坐个车回家，然后游戏就戛然而止了，中间铺垫的矛盾以及主角内心的情感都没后续啦？关于心理健康方面的探讨，作者着墨也太少了，完全可以感受到根本没放心思在上面，浪费了那么好的题材

A short and sweet adventure that is very fun and a good amount of challenge. Not nearly as punishing as hollow knight, but still fun with a few moderately difficult bosses. I really enjoyed the gameplay and how you can finish it in about 7 hours or so. Would definitely recommend.

先叠个甲，我是跳跳乐类和类银都玩的，所以游戏里出现大量跳跳乐对我来讲不算减分项目。 总体而言，这部作品我觉得可以有七分的水准，跳跳乐方面的话爬墙的手感和破黑墙（游戏内机制）的手感不是很好所以这方面扣掉一分，类银方面的话小怪和地图探索方面体验不是很好，有不少解密是没有引导的，还有就是小怪和boss设计感觉不是很上心啊，所以扣掉一分，再就是剧情方面，我想玩这款游戏有很大一部分就是因为主角的设计，但是游戏内虽然有剧情的讲解但是到最后其实都没有把整个故事讲清楚，或者说就是留白太多了，而且康斯坦斯出场次数太少根本不够看啊(┳Д┳)，所以剧情也扣掉一分，剩下的话攻击模组和角色灵活性方面设计的中规中矩，美术和音乐很优秀也有不少辅助设计（就地复原和拍照啥的），所以咱觉得这部作品还是相当有自己特色的一款游戏(｡･ω･｡)（PS：不是超级喜欢的话记得等打折，原价买这个体量不是很好）
dont know why this game has so good rezennsions, movement is far worse than in hollow knight and the boss where u have to run away in a parcour setting just was enough for me.

Em resumo, o jogo é ótimo: gameplay fluida, com uma animação linda. Todo boss é muito criativo e, apesar de muitas vezes ficar frustrado, com algumas tentativas já dá para saber o que precisa ser feito. Não dá para ficar fazendo face tank, então o que resta é aprender o padrão do boss.  Em questão de história, o jogo segue o padrão de qualidade. A forma como o tema de burnout e até mesmo a exploração no ambiente de trabalho são tratados é simplesmente perfeita, a ponto de fazer você sentir como se fosse com você. É tudo abordado de uma forma sensível e real.  Chega a ser difícil apontar algum ponto negativo sobre o jogo, de tão boa que está sendo minha experiência com ele.

Игра прям хороша, жаль, что короткая. Прям смесь Полого рыцаря и Селесты. Упор в игре идёт всё-таки на платформинг. Боссы прикольные и интересные, каждый по своему, и каждый утилизирует ваши способности, что вы только что получили.  Способностей метроидвании не много, да и то метроидвания линейная получилась, но это не страшно. Каждая вторая игра в этом жанре такая же. Да и игра короткая - не успевает надоесть.  Вот чего не хватило, так это перемещения между кострами местными. Было бы идеально прям всё что в Полом душило - тут этого почти нет.  Что ребята говорят в комментах про ту сцену с пастью - так там надо не терять времени бежать вперёд. Пасть даёт сделать всего 2-3 ошибки, потом всё - не успеть. Это, не считая секретов - самая длинная платформинг секция в игре. Разок то уж потерпеть можно.  То есть геймплейно - игра тут прям хороша.  7/10  Это пример крепкого середняка. Что-то не удалось, ну а что-то прям на хорошей высоте.  UPD. Ждём DLC

Playing Constance was like finding that one metroidvania that has all of it going for it at the start: great art and animations, good music, okay level design, but then it falls short in the long run. The boss fights and platforming are lacking in difficulty the story progression feels one-dimensional, and it just never really curves upwards in any aspect. The NPC dialog just kind of falls flat, and before you know it, the game is over.  I think Constance is a game with a lot of potential for me to have liked it because it has many of the aspects I enjoy, but sadly it just misses the mark for me and makes me feel like I wasn't able to get invested especially due to its short length.  I do, however, think this is a good game I'd recommend for people with less time or less experience to start getting into gaming. Overall, I'd recommend it for those purposes, for that audience, but not for the classic fan base that looks for more in-depth fights, platforming, etcetera.  I also feel I should mention there was frame jitter due to it not syncing correctly with my Xbox One controller, and the standard keyboard layout is ass. So even though id recommend it for people less experienced i wouldn't recommend it to the average person.

体量小到从开场动画到通关仅需6.5小时，作为快餐式类银值得品尝 [b] 优点： [/b] 1.每个BOSS都有一套特殊的解法，需要结合当前区域获取的能力，很好地创造了差 异 化的战斗体验，从初见杀到摸索解法的过程十分有趣，这一点本人相当喜欢。 2.战斗和跑酷手感都很舒服（快劈黑冲开局就送（误） 3.游戏中的各种元素与主角的画师身份结合得十分巧妙 4.地图各处都有回血小道具，甚至还有攻击回血护符，中期以后跑图就舒服了 5.死了不但能在当前房间复活而且还不掉钱 [b] 缺点： [/b] 1.刚开局在机动性不够的情况下显得格外脆皮，推图很难受。 2.剧情中对于负面情绪的描写通篇都较为浓烈，[spoiler] 到结局也只是做了短暂的“握手言和”，这很现实，公司催促和拉小提琴那块看得我头顶冒鬼火 [/spoiler]，好在明亮鲜艳的美工在一定程度上弥补了这一点，但体量小显得剧情依旧过于仓促，npc之间没什么联系，感觉就像“你说你的我说我的”。 3.血上限、蓝上限大部分属于隐藏要素，光顾着推图的话到通关可能都升级不了一次，虽然初始数值完全可以用到通关，但这样就会显得设置这些不知道到哪去找的碎片很多余 4.习惯键鼠的玩家表示鼠标上绑定了4个用于跑酷的键位，建议把下劈和戳击融合一下，单独做一个下劈出来真的显得很割裂 5.这游戏体量小得发现最终boss是缝合怪的那一刻都觉得释怀了，跑酷真没评测上说得那么难，打完有种“就这”的怅然若失

TLDR: As far as shorter metroidvanias go, this is one of the better ones you could play. Some aspects felt anticlimactic, and I wish there was one final post-game challenge, but overall, I'd highly recommend this game to fans of the genre.  I've tried a lot of smaller metroidvanias like this, and many of them I find lacking in several ways. (Not all of them can be as big and expansive as Silksong after all, though the smaller ones can still be worth playing.) Whether it's cause the combat feels clunky, the exploration feels unsatisfying, or the upgrades don't feel very powerful, there's always something that makes me want to put down the game.  Constance is one of these smaller metroidvanias, though it manages to escape all of these issues. The combat and exploration feel really good, and though the non-movement upgrades don't feel super powerful most of the time, they still feel worthwhile to pick up. Unlike other smaller metroidvanias, I never wanted to put this one down for the entire run time of the game. It's a good length for those who don't have a lot of time to put into a single game. It's short, but it doesn't feel too short. This game takes up exactly the amount of time that the story needs it too, though I do wish there was just one final post-game challenge to prove your mastery in.  The only thing I might point out is how hidden some of the optional content is. In particular, there is one specific movement upgrade that I managed to miss, reaching the end of the game without it. While this isn't necessarily a bad thing, I did end up finding ways to complete certain platforming puzzles without this movement upgrade, simply because I didn't realize that there was an easier way.  The point is, I tend to be pretty thorough in my exploration in these types of games, but due to the very hidden nature of the optional content, and the fact that I was missing a movement upgrade for most of the game, I ended up obtaining many upgrades and abilities long after they could be of any use to me. All bosses were already beaten, and all challenges were already completed. I still find it fun to do the final bits of completion like this in any game, but completion did feel somewhat anticlimactic in that way. Again, I do wish there was one final post-game challenge.  The emotional beats of the game were done pretty well. Nothing quite brought me to tears, but with how sparse the story telling is, and how little you learn about the character of Constance, you get a pretty good glimpse into her headspace, and really start to feel for her. Similar to the anticlimax of the completion though, I do feel like the story and emotion could have used just a little bit more at the end there. Maybe just another minute or two of ending cutscene would've done it for me. I appreciate a good story told through sparse moments and environmental details, but there is such thing as not enough detail, and I think this game needs just a tiny bit more.  Overall though, I found this game to be extremely enjoyable. I highly recommend it, especially for fans of similar games in the genre, like Hollow Knight, or Momodora.

以前的新品节玩过demo，后来虽然首发入手了，但是鉴于当时的评价，等到了最近的1.1大更新才开始玩。 游戏本身设计了一些特色，无论是美术以及一些功能性设计并不算凡庸，然而跳跳乐以及敌人设计水平则无法匹配属实遗憾。  本作流程以颜料和艺术创作作为主题，无论是利用颜料的技能或是关于创作的一些相关设计，例如失真和拍照功能等确实是比较亮眼的。敌人也有相关的主题设计，包括绘画和雕塑等，整个画面也是色彩丰富，总体来说把这个主题利用得比较细致。本作的地图不仅能一键打开聚焦小范围的小型地图，还能快速进行地图的移动操作。而且前期很快就会解锁快照功能，能将某个地点的场景记录下来作为地图上的标记，比起类银常规的标记点功能可以说是高级不少。本作也没有死亡惩罚。  相比之下，敌人的核心设计就水平很低。平常小怪有碰撞伤害，以及阴险站位（例如悬崖上有能够快速转向的盾牌哥）都不说了，BOSS战设计就很无聊。尤其是前两场BOSS都是强制无法输出，让你要像马戏团一样耍猴戏一直躲伤害之类的，等好久让你能够平A打几下，接着又开始猴戏，如此循环。第一次BOSS我也就忍了，结果第二场还是很关键的BOSS战居然还是这么玩，只能说设计水平已经到头了。BOSS战一点互动都没有，硬拖时间。 其他的话比如篝火数量分布太少，即使路上给了回血的罐子也严重不足。本作也没有血瓶一说，还需要平常满生命值额外获得的治疗（也就是打碎罐子）才能存起来（也只有一点点）。虽然死亡的时候给了选项可以在本房间继续，但是代价就是小怪全部加强，要坐篝火取消效果。篝火少也意味着跑路就要很久了。而且也没有给传送功能，只有一个电梯能用，电梯也只能去垂直方向上指定的几个地点。跳跳乐就是操作很繁杂，不少技能都要配合各种按键施展。即使是初期消除失真的技能又跳又劈砍还要赶时间也挺烦，包括几个限制时间内点灯开门的地方。  本作实际上还有一个心理健康主题，即女主现实中生活压力大。结果玩家也是被搞得有点压力大，还是有点难绷的。无论是艺术创作主题还是这个工作压力的问题，其实都有发挥空间。本作有一定的底子在，但是没有真正意义上做得比较好。就个人而言，与其让玩家也去强制感受那种痛苦，不如把游戏多打磨一点，做得更放松一些。主要是敌人要设计得更合理一些，不只是说难度大不大的问题，包括像BOSS战这种拖时间缺乏互动的设计对玩家而言体验是很差的。

Great game. Sort of Hollow Knight with Celeste theming, but it has its own unique identity.  Great: - Art, the visuals are expressive and well done, and each area feels unique - Exploration, loved exploring and filling out the map. Secrets are fun to find. - Platforming, movement is tight and fluid. Optional platforming content was pretty difficult.  Good: - Bosses, most bosses were very fun if a bit procedural. A couple felt like they could be tuned a little more. - Enemies, enemy variety is pretty solid and creative for how long this game is. Nothing too difficult but fun to fight. - Music, the soundtracks fit the areas well and were enjoyable to have on in the background.  Neutral: - Narrative, story was decent although it was mostly delivered in interludes that had little relation to actual gameplay, and was maybe a little heavy-handed.  Bad: - Length, game could be longer. It's a good game when this is my only major complaint.  Overall I enjoyed this a ton, and it scratched the same itch that Hollow Knight and Silksong did. Highly recommend picking up and playing now that patch 1.1 is out.

While playing this game you can definetly tell that the devs loved this game, the art is incredible, the bossfights are creative, the platforming sections are great, the only negatives are that the game is a little too easy (i know its subjective and its fine that the game has this difficulty, it just drags my opinion a little lower) and that some mechanics are easy to abuse to cheese your way into places you shouldnt be able to, especially with the clone/ pogo abilities (how to abuse these abilities: [spoiler] go against a wall and pogo your clone, in the air you can place a new clone and instantly pogo again [/spoiler]). But since i didnt encounter any game breaking situation or made it way to easy, i will see it as a positive, since i had a lot of fun trying to cheese my way in the platforming sections where i was supposed to have the [spoiler] double jump [/spoiler] but was using the clone/ pogo combo instead.  In general great game, i would definetly recommend, its worth the 20€ especially now that it is 20% off (15€)

[h1]Primeras impresiones[/h1] Adquirí Constance porque al haber jugado anteriormente otro juego del género [b]metroidvania[/b] (Hollow Knight: Silksong), supe que este me gustaría también. No escuché tanto de este juego como de otros del mismo género; sin embargo, al haber visto el diseño de los personajes, mapas, etc., las habilidades del personaje y las mecánicas del juego en el tráiler del mismo, más el dato de que algunos grandes juegos suelen pasar desapercibidos al principio, decidí sin vacilación que quería jugar Constance.  [h1]Jugabilidad[/h1] Sencillo, entretenido, no es muy largo para terminar, y posee una historia muy interesante. La banda sonora también es agradable e incluso relajante, al igual que los diálogos de los personajes. Los ataques y movimientos del personaje, junto con sus inspiraciones y el relativamente bajo nivel de habilidad requerido para utilizar correctamente el personaje y sus mecánicas, lo hacen deseable de jugar.  [h1]Opinión + dato a recalcar[/h1] Personalmente, lo que más me gustó de este videojuego no fue la sencillez para jugarlo y completarlo, las mecánicas, la música, ni ningún otro de esos elementos típicos, sino, más bien, el [b]acompañamiento emocional[/b] y cada [b]bello e inspirador mensaje[/b] mostrados en cada situación particular desde el inicio del juego hasta el final del mismo. No solo es la jugabilidad y los diálogos de los personajes; es, más que nada, una historia con diversas escenas donde un jugador que atraviesa problemas de tipo emocional consigo mismo, tales como [b]culpa, vergüenza, baja autoestima, sensación de insuficiencia, ansiedad, tristeza, miedo e incluso depresión[/b], tranquilamente puede identificarse con la protagonista. Desde el inicio hasta el final, la protagonista, de nombre Constance, atraviesa una profunda lucha emocional consigo misma. El foco de este videojuego, a diferencia de muchos otros de género metroidvania, no es en derrotar enemigos y jefes por todo el mapa, sino en enfrentar tus propios miedos, dudas, y todo aquello que malogre tu ánimo, autoestima y capacidad para salir adelante. Sí, hay enemigos y jefes por derrotar, pero estos se muestran en un plano emocional más que en un plano físico. A lo largo de la historia se ve cómo cada personaje tiene un problema que resolver, a quienes Constance debe ayudar, y en el proceso de resolución de estos problemas se ven mensajes alentadores que no solo van para el personaje, sino también, de forma implícita, para el jugador.  [h1]Recomendaciones[/h1] Ayer terminé el juego y hoy lo completé al 100%, por lo que ya puedo emitir una recomendación más acertada. En el plano puramente lúdico, recomiendo Constance a quienes quieran un videojuego que sea fácil y con una historia no muy larga.  En el plano emocional, recomiendo enormemente este videojuego a quienes atraviesan condiciones y problemas emocionales consigo mismo y que necesitan validación, puesto que a lo largo de la historia se muestran mensajes y enseñanzas totalmente positivas y alentadoras que motivan al jugador a luchar y seguir adelante, además de que el jugador puede sentirse visto y acompañado al notar que con alguna situación del juego puede llegar a pensar "Eh, esto se parece a lo que me pasa a mí".

The core gameplay is where it really shines, though the abilities and boss fights are incredibly fun to play through. A huge plus is the accessibility; it's not too difficult, making it a great choice for players who find the Metroidvania genre a bit intimidating. Cons: The main downside is the length, the game is quite short and left me wanting more. Additionally, the side characters feel a bit thin, they could have used more depth or interaction. Final Thoughts If you enjoyed Hollow Knight, this is a must buy. It carries that same DNA but offers a smoother experience for less hardcore players. It's a solid title at full price, but if you see it on sale, don't even think twice just grab it.

Just finished the game, and just wanted to write out the many thoughts I had while playing (mild spoilers ahead):  I really loved the game! I especially liked how they took established mechanics of the Metroidvania genre and innovated on them. The inspiration mechanic, for instance, is the coolest I have seen, and I have played quite a few Metroidvanias. Having not just the cost, but unique shapes to worry about and arrange as well, gives the mechanic even more depth. Those inspirations that power up the more inspirations surround them, is another fantastic idea.  Another mechanic that was superbly implemented is the photographs mechanic. In other Metroidvanias, you have pins which you place, and after a while, you will have completely forgotten why you marked the location in the first place. Taking pictures of your screen and using those as pins is such a better approach that I keep wondering why nobody else has done it yet, I haven't seen it, at least. The world of Constance is stunning and always a pleasure to look at. The artists did a fantastic job. The different characters are all unique and offer interesting quests with rewarding... rewards.  The narrative and story are well thought out. While oftentimes obvious in which direction it will ultimately go, that didn't diminish the fact that Constance's struggles and hardships felt real. I truly had goosebumps in the ending sequence. I lastly want to touch upon the gameplay. Overall, the gameplay is very well done. Combat feels tactical. With many different attacks and inspirations, there are a lot of possibilities to deal the most damage possible, which feels incredibly satisfying. The movement abilities are, overall, also great. My favourite one is definitely the clone ability. It literally allows you to gain infinite flight, climb walls, and much more. I highly appreciate that the developers kept this in the game and let the players have fun with the mechanic! So, this was a lot of praise until now. That's why I also want to touch upon a couple of things I didn't enjoy as much. Firstly, I think a lot of bosses are unclear in how you're supposed to defeat them. Take The Manipulator, for example. He is the boss you fight right after you obtain the pogo ability. In his first phase, he spawns a balloon bunny, among other balloons. The intended way is to completely destroy the bunny, upon which the phase will change. Me, not realizing this, kept trying to continuously pogo off of the balloons the boss spawns, to gain enough elevation to hit him. That is quite tough to do. Only after 5 or 6 attempts did I happen to kill the bunny, upon which the phase changed. There is no indication that you have to kill the bunny whatsoever, as many other balloons keep spawning as well. And also considering you fight this boss 3 minutes after obtaining the pogo ability, it's not unreasonable to think that players will attempt to kill the boss the described way. (Also, on a side note, I encountered a bug here. I think I defeated the boss right as the boss was changing from his stunned phase to another one, and the game soft locked. That's the only bug I ever encountered, though, I think). Other bosses have similar problems with not quickly communicating how to defeat them. Cornelis, in the same area, has a phase where he spins around. He cannot be damaged from the side. You have to figure out that you can only damage him from above. But trying to pogo him, you find that he will hit you every time, as his height is just too big to directly bounce on him. You have to bounce on separate spikes, then, and only then, damage the boss. In my opinion, the boss should be taller, or at least the spinning attack should be, to clearly convey that you definitely cannot directly bounce onto him. The spikes, as a side note, also spawn too quickly and are only telegraphed by subtle glowing. It's not unreasonable that you will not see that a spike is about to emerge inside of you in the heat of battle.  These were just a couple examples, but many bosses have similar issues. Secondly, another grievance I have is the dash. In general, your dash is invincible. However, there are so many exceptions to this that you internally cannot rely on this whatsoever. Yellow, corrupted enemies cannot be dashed through. Fine, that's their whole point. But then there is the black corruption, that you can't dash through, either. Then, there is blue electricity, that ALSO cannot be dashed through. But on the other side, there are yellow designed enemies and attacks in the game that you CAN dash through, despite your instinct telling you yellow = no dash through. These inconsistencies make it hard to fully trust your dash in combat. Another nitpick I have: when you dash off of a ledge, you do an airborne dash. You don't even have to be close to the ledge. That feels very weird. You're dashing repeatedly along the ground, and all of a sudden, you do an air dash, which gets rid of your coyote frames and therefore your possibility to jump, just because a ledge was in your close proximity. That, again, makes your dash feel like an unreliable tool, that sometimes does things out of your control. While this last paragraph did sound quite negative, I do want to reiterate that these issues are only small grievances, and overall, the great upsides of Constance far outweigh these flaws. Constance is a great game with stunning visuals, great narrative and fun mechanics. I highly recommend anyone to give it a go. And in case any human soul read this to the end and has been wondering: Yes, this all was written by me and me alone.

Eğer iyi yada kötü şıkkının yanında nötr olsaydı onu verirdim. Oyun güzel ancak çok fazla olumsuz yan vardı. İyi ve kötü yönleri sıralayacak olursak:  **** Kötü Yanları **** -> Oyunda checkpointlerin yeri bazı yerlerde saçma olmuş. Boss öldürdükten sonra tekrardan yanına gidebilmek için çok çabalamak gerekiyor. Aradaki sürede soğuyorsunuz. -> Oyun beklediğimden çook kısa sürdü. 8 saat gibi bir sürede ana hikaye bitiyor. Parası çok değil oyunun ama yine de bana kısa geldi. Oyunu tam başarım yapmak isterseniz uzar süre ama başarımlar casual bi oyuncu için fazla zor çabalamak için hiçbir sebep bulamadım. -> Oyunda daha açılmayan 2 tane yetenekle oyun bitti ki internetten gördüğüm kadarıyla bunlardan biri de metroidvania oyunlarının olmazsa olması double jump. Yani çok mantıksız nasıl olabilir. -> Boya sistemi bok gibi olmuş. Parkur yapmak için kullanılan yeteneklerin nerdeyse hepsi boya harcıyor ve havada boyasız kalıyorsunuz. Çok can sıkıyor endgame kısımlarında. -> Hikayesi çok yavandı. Bir şey anlatmak istemişler ama tam anlatamamışlar gibi hissettirdi.  **** İyi Yanları **** -> Oyunda ölünce check pointten mi yoksa ceza ile olduğun yerden mi doğmak istersin opsiyonu güzel olmuş. Bazı yerlerden keyfin kaçmasını engelliyor. Hem de kolaylaştırmıyor çokta oyunu. ->  Harita güzel dizayn edilmiş. Çok kafa karıştırmayan güzel çok büyük olmayan bir harita. -> Oyunun açık ara en iyi yaptığı şey map kontolü. Böyle oyunlarda map kontrol etmek gerçekten çok kritik ve bu sistemi aşırı iyi yapmışlar. Keşif yaparken hiç daralmadım sayesinde.  -> Oyunun Türkçe dil desteği olması belki de bana olumlu yorum yaptıran şey oldu yoksa bu kadar olumsuz özellik içeren oyuna normalde beğendim demezdim. Artık bunun normalleşmesi lazım  küçük yapımcılar bile yapıyor yani. -> Sanat tasarımını beğendim. Atmosfer de fena tasarlanmamış.  Özetle oyun arada bir deneyim sundu. İyi ki indirimden almışım yoksa indirimsiz fiyatını çokta hak ettiğini söyleyemem. Oyunun 2. oyunu çıkacaksa bunlara dikkat ederlerse çok daha güzel bir oyun ortaya çıkar.  PUANIM: 2.5/5

A beautifully drawn and animated metroidvania that could stand to be a bit longer. Touches on themes of burnout, depression, and belief in self in a poignant way. Music is quite good.  Gameplay is fluid and feels good; the bosses are mechanically interesting to solve; there are a few sections here and there where some balancing might be needed (Giant Maw Chase, in particular); the platforming - while never impossibly difficult - is precise and sometimes frustrating. The map is barebones but functional; the exploration was fun; and the side quests are interesting and varied.  First run to 100% took around 12h. Then 6h more to mop up the speed-run related achievements. All in all, a solid entry to the genre.

A beautiful and engaging Metroidvania, it took me about 20 hours to achieve 100% completion of the game (though not all achievements).  Pros: - Beautiful art and music - Diverse abilities, bosses, and platforming challenges keep things interesting - Side quests add life to the story with unique NPC interactions - The majority of the most difficult platforming is optional, and feels really satisfying to master - Accessibility options help reduce the difficulty and frustration when needed - Completion indicaters per region/room closer to the end of the game  Cons: - The story behind the titular character feels like it could be fleshed out a little more. I feel like I'm missing a connection between Celeste's real-life experiences and the painted world she's traversing - Jumping/pogoing feels a little sluggish - One ability is missable, and it would have been useful to find sooner to facilitate 100% completion - One required timed platforming section feels particularly unforgiving, with an unstoppable lengthy animation sequence at the beginning that feels time-wasting after the nth attempt. Suggest allowing the animation sequence to be skipped, and possibly introducing an accessibility setting that slows the time-limiting element, so players with mobility challenges aren't prevented from finishing the game
