[h2] Score: 6.5/10 [/h2]  PROS:  - pleasant art style, even though not all NPC are equally good implemented (this elevator NPCs somehow annoying) - developers tried to create new gameplay mechanics, but not all of them quite suitable or work well. However, I want to praise and encourage developers: their creativity is a key and they are doing good. Keep it up. It is precious nowadays when artists/developers does not simply copy/paste but try to find new approaches to every aspect of the game - sound is ok, but somehow does not click for me (it is subjective of course. Probably my expectations impacted by CO: Expedition 33). - secrets and hidden area nice to explore. Except hitboxes issue, most platforming challenges are good.   I've played game before any QoL patches this is in roadmap for early 2026. So maybe some of these issues below are already fixed and final score could be higher. CONS:  - By itself game is not difficult intentionally (except few platforming challenges), but the "main boss" is very difficult. Boss name is hitboxes and control locked animations. I swear in this rather cute game I was swearing like the last cobbler cursing these stupid hitboxes. - Map is ok, every room has correct shape. So I do not get why Constance pin is not properly positioned within the room. Instead it is simply pinned at center-top of the room. There are also bug where few paths are not shown on the map. I spend a lot of time searching lower part of room for secret, while there were entrance to other part of chamber which simply was not shown on map. - I did not quite like the "cube" mechanics. Personally it feels not related to game. And game never tells you that you have to dodge into cube, so you enter and become one with a cube. Yes, literally. And yes, it is weird.   - "Electric cable" quest. Another thing that game never tell you. You simply have to guess somehow that performing directional brush attacks in same direction of cable somehow supposed to fix it. This is strange AF.  - Game is a 20% cheaper that HK: Silksong, but has apporx. 15-25% content of HK: Sinksong. It does not bother me much personally, though. Just letting you know.   VERDICT: Small but beautiful game for few evenings. If it is remembered, it is only because of the art style.
Ce jeu est vraiment magnifique. Il tombait à point nommé avec ce que je traverse en ce moment.

Constance è un buon gioco, è fatto bene e si vede la passione degli sviluppatori dietro. Putroppo non offre un contenuto sufficiente per il suo prezzo e questo è il motivo principale per cui non mi sento di consigliarlo se non acquistato a un grosso sconto quando non si ha nient'altro da giocare. Soprattuto quando il tempo che ci ho messo a finire Constance è lo stesso che ci ho messo a finire la demo di Mio: memories in orbit.  TLDR: piccolezze e superficialità rendono l'esperienza meno godibile di quanto potrebbe essere, il gioco si sforza troppo di apparire "diverso dalle masse" dimenticandosi di ciò che contraddistingue il genere metroidvania.  Constance è quel gioco che si comincia quando ti sei spolpato un silksong o un nine sols, sperando che ti riempia, almeno per poco, quella voglia di metroidvania che ti solletica il cervello ma fallisce nel farlo. Lo stile grafico è ottimo e la musica ispirata (soprattutto quella del boss finale), ma il mondo manca di una lore che ti faccia appassionare, la trama sulla depressione, ormai argomento trito e ritrito, cade piatta non riuscendo mai a farti empatizzare con il personaggio principale né a farti affezionare ai pochi NPC che si incontrano durante il viaggio e il mondo è vuoto di segreti. Sì c'è qualche parete nascosta con della valuta dentro, ma niente che possa essere definito emozionante.  A livello di gameplay è decisamente valido nonostante diverse scelte che mi hanno lasciato interdetto: perché il wall jump (wall slide?) va attivato col tasto dello scatto? perché [spoiler]il pogo[/spoiler] è un'intera abilità che va sbloccata a parte e che ha un pulsante dedicato? e molti altri piccolissimi dettagli che non mi metterò ad elencare, ma che insieme rendono l'esperienza di gioco meno piacevole. Certo possono essere scelte stilistiche, ma ogni volta mi è sembrato un modo per far vedere "quanto siamo diversi". Le altre recensioni parlano già abbastanza della mappa di gioco, bella la meccanica delle diapositive, ma tutto il resto è insufficiente. Spero la rendano funzionale negli aggiornamenti successivi.  Una meccanica davvero originale che ho apprezzato moltissimo è la meccanica della morte. Lasciare da parte la classica corpse run in cambio di questa è stata un'ottima idea, poter scegliere di continuare da dove si è morti (circa) in cambio di nemici più potenti e coriacei è geniale, ma anche qui forse a causa dell'enorme paura degli sviluppatori di rendere l'esperienza troppo frustrante (che si percepisce per l'intero gioco, soprattutto nella fase finale) per ogni nemico base che si sconfigge si viene premiati con il doppio della valuta di gioco + cura, questa "modalità difficile" rende il gioco ancora più semplice e indulgente lanciando ogni sensazione di sfida fuori dalla finestra. Fortunatamente il lato platformer è incredibilmente valido, raggiungendo alcuni picchi davvero soddisfacenti ed è, senza ombra di dubbio, la parte più valida dell'intero gioco. Una volta presa la mano con i comandi poco ortodossi l'esperienza è scattante e responsiva, purtroppo queste sezioni sono davvero corte e con ricompense alla fine non all'altezza, ma ho trovato le sezioni di platform abbastanza soddisfacenti.  Credevo tanto in Constance prima della sua uscita, ma si è rivelato una delusione. Avrei ancora tanto da dire, ma la recensione risulterebbe davvero troppo lunga. Peccato, poteva essere qualcosa di davvero grande.

Constance is a short and a fun game. It has it's flaws but the inherent game is amazing. A good 8/10.  My biggest problem is this game kinda starts having an identity crisis in the middle on what it wants to be. It tries to take inspiration from many games; but forgets what it wants to be.  - The artstyle and the music in the game is nice. - You have a solid Metroidvania; But the game suddenly adds animations of the theme it's trying to push. Like you get a hints from the environment but suddenly it's right there on your face. - The jump in difficulty between the normal gameplay and bosses is insane. - You have some pretty cool platforming challenges, but it's annoying because of one mechanic ( I'll mention it below ).  The rant, Note: Like most games nowadays, this game looks like it had a hurried release cycle. Causing it to have a lot of rough edges, which will require and lot of patches to be fixed. [spoiler]  There is no fast travel, I initially assumed it'd be an unlock; Because there are resting site spread across some location of the map; but no. I think they ran out of time to implement that feature and that makes It's really annoying navigating the whole map. The same case is with the tutorial sections, most initial mechanics have very good levels and information on how to use them. But as it reaches the end, the pacing is kind off abrupt. I hate, I mean hate the way the wall climb skill is implemented. You sometimes have to double dash to wall climb and sometimes it's just a single dash. Moreover you have to dash again to escape the wall climb. Causing a lot of headaches in some sections. I liked how the plunge had a separate button, but why does it work even with the attack button. It completely messes with your attack combos. Causing you to down dash during some boss fights and loss a lot of progress. ( The Manipulator boss fight was mess because of this ).  [/spoiler]

It's very well done!  There are some harder platforming parts which can be annoying, but to a healthy level. The game doesn't hate you! Unlike a lot of other hyped ones, which know no balance, just give on-hit bosses to struggle with, etc. The boss battles are nicely learnable here. The abilities are also given at a nice pace.  Also there is a good respawn system, so that you can choose continue from where you failed with some handicap, so there is no real punishment.  I would have loved an option to have some tint setting for the paint :) - but I guess there'd be problems with the general game colors.  I could complain about it being a bit short, but I won't. Good job, thanks for the game!

Other reviews imo overhate on this game for the crime of not being Silksong. But this game has its own unique ideas and good points that make it a solid experience for metroidvania (not JUST Silksong/Hollow Knight) fans.  Here's some things it has to offer: - The game is visually beautiful - Good OST with a few standout songs - It feels good/smooth to control for the most part (the wall climb takes some getting used to)  - There are some moderately difficult fights/platforming sections, so I wouldn't say the game is too easy  - The story won't resonate with everyone but I think it's done well and pretty personal  Some cons: - The combat could use some more depth as there is little in the way of having different builds/playstyles (this is not necessary for such a short game though).  - I wouldn't have minded some harder late game boss content - The game is just small in scope overall. It's only like 8-9 hours to 100%, though they do have challenges to speedrun the game. If you view the value of a game in terms of number of hours per dollar spent, it would be low (though I think that mindset just leads you to getting your time wasted by bloated games).  Overall, I think this is a good experience if you like metroidvanias as a genre and I'd recommend it, though maybe wait for a sale if you don't feel like a ~10 hour game is worth $20.

[b] 质量上还是不错的类银恶游戏，可以推荐。 [/b] 各方面都中规中矩。人物的能力比较丰富，探图方式会比较多样，不过也会带来常用按键过多的问题。boss战方面大部分都是经典的躲一套打一套，摸清套路后都不难。跳跳乐的难度也比较正常，没有太过令人捉急的地方，唯一要吐槽的话可能是这个尖刺的判定比视觉看起来要大很多，尤其是上下方向的尖刺，经常会出现明明人物跳起来还没碰到尖刺却被吸过去了的情况.... 地图设计上比较一般，部分房间之间太过相似了，容易导致迷路。再加上游戏的地图查看功能也不怎么实用，一方面是只显示大房间没法查看细节位置，进房间后连自己在哪个角落都不知道，尤其在巨型房间；另一方面是这个地图的查看控制也很别扭，无论是移动、放大、看照片等等操作都很奇怪。不过更新后新加入的收集度显示倒是很实用。 [b] 可惜的是这个作品的流程还是太短了 [/b] 不算支线和收集的话，不用几个小时就能通关主线。整体质量不错的情况下，却也说不上是短小精悍，更像是没有做完的感觉，缺少一个高潮。[b] 只能给一个下限较高但上限却不太行的评价了，可惜。 [/b] ———————————————— 1.1更新改进了地图显示，还没体验，使用上应该会比较舒服了。

Yes, I finished the game and had a lot of fun doing it. I would recommend.  Pros:  worth 20 dollars Amazing art Amazing story Fun combat Unique boss designs and fights No bugs or game breaking problems i came across No annoying runbacks The game soundtrack is amazing  Cons: Getting upgrades to your character aka extra health and extra paint usage feels optional.  The movement or upgrades feel really region specific. I used the clone ability 2 times the entire playthrough. the wall jump ability does not work on almost any of the normal areas besides the place you get it.  all of the bosses are really easy. the most i ever died to a boss is 6 times and it was on the joker card fella. once you memorize every attack its just a cakewalk.  there is no good way to traverse the map. yes there is the fast travel but sometimes you need to go someplace to do a quest and it takes forever to get there.  overall I feel like the map is good, but needs work.  characters have not much dialogue, I liked some of the characters but they felt dead when reading the text. they don't really have an identity behind the design or the sounds they make when they talk.  Overall the game is totally worth the 20 dollars, just some small problems that I found. I wish there were some changes to the map and the characters but other then that amazing game. Would definitely play again for fun.

Constance as a game isn't particularly subtle about its themes or its message, but that's not necessarily a bad thing. Its inspirations are pretty transparent as well, both in gameplay, concept and general storytelling. So then what are we left with? A decent game that respects your time that's surprisingly fun for how short I'm selling it right now. The music is great, the art direction is amazing and the areas all feel unique and fun to traverse. Constance also does something that almost no other game did for me, in providing me with a visually memorable enemy cast. I usually don't look twice at enemies in games, as I'm generally never fond of their looks, but I could tell you about every enemy I encountered in Constance in detail when it comes to their looks, the designs are genuinely fantastic. The boss fights are also a highlight of this game. They're all what you'd call "gimmicky" but that's exactly why I enjoyed them, it made them feel unique and while the solution to each one was "hit them until they're dead", the roundabout ways that the game makes you take to get there were really fun to dissect and understand (♥♥♥♥ the chase boss though). Overall Constance is a game I'd definitely recommend to people who like action platformers and want a blend of cozy atmosphere and challenging sequences.

Ну, что тут сказать. Мне, если честно, напомнило смесь Hollow Knight: Silksong и Celeste, но игра очень здоровская и приятная как по части рисовки, так и по геймплею. Локации красочные, интересные, очень понравилось их исследовать. Платформинг присутствует, и не мало, а также не назову его слишком лёгким или слишком сложным, что, несомненно, плюс [spoiler] кроме задания по сбору коктейлей, вот там мне в некоторых местах пришлось попотеть, потом пальцы дымились [/spoiler]. Радует разнообразие мобов и их атак. В каждой части карты свой набор врагов, подходящих к ней по стилю (что тоже отнесём в +). Боссы интересные, у каждого свой стиль боя и индивидуальные патерны атак (+). Что же касаемо сюжета? Сюжет интересный, но, как мне кажется, раскрыт странно. В описании сказано, что мы как бы побеждаем свои психологические травмы, но тогда причём тут Сэр [strike]сопля[/strike] Рыголот? Или что за травма такая, из-за которой нам нужно убегать от космического чудовища со ртом во весь экран? Вот это непонятно Что ещё хочу отнести к минусам? Лечение в бою. Оно сделано очень непривычно, ведь не набирается со временем и даже не набивается, когда ты бьёшь боссов или мобьё. Чтобы накопить хотя бы на одно лечение, тебе нужно разбить несколько предметов (причём эти предметы нужно сначала найти, ведь не в каждой комнате они есть). Как вы понимаете, из-за этого на первых стадиях игры (в последствии можно увеличить количество ячеек хила до 3-х, но их всё равно нужно ещё заполнять), да и чего скрывать, на поздних, чаще всего, тоже, вам желательно идти на босса, предварительно по максимуму заполнив все лечилки, которые у вас есть, т.к. на арене вам хрен кто даст эти предметы для хила. Лично я прошёл игру на 100% (чему сам удивлён, не ожидал от себя, особенно по части платформинга) и остался доволен. Несмотря на все минусы, игра ОЧЕНЬ крутая и интересная. This is a pretty short game, although what it has is pretty well put together, it left me wanting more because there were so many systems I wish I had more time to play with! The boss fights are great and the art theme is used in unique in fun ways. In some parts it felt *too inspired* by hollow knight, because the inspiration is clearly there, but later in the game it gains its own identity more, especially in terms of moveset!! The movement was very well designed and without going into detail, the game has maybe the most fun upgrade system I've experienced in a metroidvania the story is a bit simple and maybe a tad cliche? but done well enough and did in my opinion a good job at making you care about the protagonist, not so much about the side characters but that's given for such a short game. I've had to use a walkthrough in 3 points to get 100% completion once I reached 94%, so that's not too bad! All in all I give Constance an 8/10. :D

Constance is a great game! For anyone that loves metroidvanias I suggest it.  It also has some really creative boss fights and offers a beautiful background and art style. It is just really really wholesome!  Gamemechanics:  I like the paint system. It restricts your moves like silk in silksong, but you can use them way more. This is because you lose hp, when you use a move and your paint is gone. So it isn't the hardest game but makes you think twice about using a ability. If you try to speedrun it, it becomes hard, because the last boss is hard without a dobble jump. But if you do a normal run and end up at like 70% game completion. You can expect to destroy the final boss and end up with like 7h playtime.  It took me 17h to get all achivements in this game so it isn't really big. I hope that it will offer a lot of more content in the future.  Small thing I don't like:  The only thing that I really don't like is the drop rate of Glimmer(Money). For my 100% run I was left with 5 items to buy from the shop to get 100%. Because of that I needed to grind money for 25 minutes, which really anoyed me. This is because I did everything in the game and money grinding is: Go up to 2 enemys kill them and then die to respawn||repeat.   Still anyone that likes Silksong or any 2d game with a great art style: GO AND GET IT!

This game is an incredible Metroidvania that I would absolutely recommend to everyone, I just now got the final achievement and I gotta say, overall, I really really enjoyed the game, my only complaint is that I wish there was more game.  The artstyle and music are both fire and the gameplay is awesome, it takes the classic (or I suppose modern?) Metroidvania formula and reimagines a lot of the tools available to the player within it's own twists, sure, some of these will take a bit of time to get used to (looking at the Mantis Claw equivalent) but they honestly are awesome when getting the grasp of them  The majority of bosses in this game are very creative and interesting, and I would say, as a person that's played Hollow Knight, Silksong, Both Ori games and Nine Sols to 100% completion, this game's final boss is somewhere up on my top 5 favorite Metroidvania bosses (so far) It's really GOOD and the music is incredible.  The game also offers a good set of platforming challenges which I found took me a few attempts to beat using Constance's unique toolkit to create some equally unique platforming sections  I am not sure if the people behind the game are planning to add DLC in the future, but I just wanna say, I think you made something really special and I'd love to experience more of the game in the future (Or something like a Godhome/Battle Memories equivalent)

動画版レビュー↓https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=g4Y4qhh6Wc4 Flydigi Direwolf 2コントローラーを使用。 [hr][/hr]  [h1]あらすじとゲーム概要[/h1] ◆ ホロウナイト系のゲームプレイが特徴的な、滑らかなアートスタイルのメトロイドヴァニア系探索型アクションプラットフォーマー作品。  主人公である若き芸術家「コンスタンス」は、かつては情熱を持って絵を描いていたものの、様々な出来事が重なり、それらがきっかけとなり、創作意欲が急激に低下し、キャンバスを前にしても何も描けなくなってしまう。  焦り、孤独、自己否定が積み重なり、主人公は、ついには心の内側に広がる“歪んだ精神世界”へと迷い込んでしまう。 その世界は表面的にはカラフルで美しいものの、彼女の精神状態の象徴となる敵や仕掛けが待ち受けている。  コンスタンスは絵筆(ブラシ)と絵の具（インク）の力を使い、自身の内なる“恐怖”と向き合い、複雑に絡み合った精神世界から脱出を目指す事となる。  本作は先ほど述べたように、主人公の内なる葛藤やトラウマをテーマとしており、燃え尽き症候群、焦り、不安、自己嫌悪など、様々な精神的な“課題”が直接的に、あるいは抽象的に表現されていく。  これらはボス戦の後や重要なアイテム入手後など、物語が進むと共にカットシーンやちょっとしたミニゲームとして表現されていき、現実での過去、出来事を見る事ができる。  また、そういった直接的な物語だけでなく、主人公が訪れることとなるマップはビビッドな色使いでありながらも、何処か毒々しい色使いを使用しており、ステージや背景の表現にも主人公の精神状態が反映されている点が面白い。  登場する敵、特にボス戦はこれらのトラウマが上手く表現されているので、気になった方は考察して見るとそちらの面でも楽しめるかもしれない。  ネタバレを避けるために一か所だけ例えとして紹介すると、本が乱雑に、複雑に配置された図書館が登場し、一部が宇宙の様な背景となっており、さらにとある敵に“飲み込まれる”“吸い込まれる”という表現がある事から、こちらは所謂「オーバーティンキング」、物事や悩みを必要以上に深く、ネガティブに考えすぎてしまう「思考の無限ループ」、悪い意味での「考えすぎ」、悪い思考に“飲み込まれる”などを表現したものとなっている可能性が高い。  また、これらの表現を引き立ててくれるBGM周りの作りも非常に上手いのだが、一つ欠点があり、SEや他の音に対して全体的にBGMが低めに設定されている。  こちらに関してはプレイ環境によって差もあるが、BGM以外の音量をオプションで下げ、プレイしている環境（PCであればイヤホンやスピーカーなど）の音量を上げる事で対策できるので、困ったらやってみて欲しい。  アクション面では「ホロウナイト」からの影響が強いモノとなっているが、絵筆（ブラシ）とインクを使った独特なモノとなっている。  こちらに関しては次の項目から詳しく解説していこう。   [h1]アクションと戦闘[/h1] ◆ 主人公の使用する武器がブラシであるものの、他の項目でも述べたように、本作は全体的に「ホロウナイト」からの影響を受けており、特にそれはアクション面に現れている。  影響元の特徴の一つでもあるアクション、敵やトゲなどの特定のトラップを頭上から攻撃する事によって自身を跳ね上げることができる「ポゴ」等も取り入れている。  だが、単にそのまま特徴的なアクションを取り入れているわけではなく、本作では“スタミナ”のように機能、時間と共に自動で回復する「インクゲージ」が存在し、特定の技を発動させるために必要となる。  本作の面白い点として、「インクゲージ」が切れている場合でも技自体は発動可能となっている事が挙げられる。 ただし、ゲージが切れている状態で使用した場合はHPが代わりに消費される。  そのため、特定の強力なアクション、本作では「ブラシテクニック」と呼ばれる技を連発出来ない事によって、戦闘での駆け引きが生まれる。  特に無敵時間有りのダッシュ、回避行動はインクゲージを消費するため、ボス戦等でパニックを起こして連打すると酷い目にあったりする。  ボス戦と言えば、本作はボス戦に非常に力を入れており、比較的多めに発生する。  参考までにいうと、大抵のエリアでは、中ボスが2体、大ボスが1体というような形だ。  本作でのボス戦、正確にはボスのパターンは上手く作られている。 挑戦的でありながらも、理不尽ではない攻撃、プレイヤーが自身の取れる行動を理解する事でノーダメージで撃破する事も勿論できる。  少し特殊なのが、大抵は敵の攻撃を数回避けていくと、敵が大きなスキを見せるので、そのタイミングでこちらが攻撃を叩き込む形になっている点だ。  その分、後半は敵の攻撃は激しめになる。 そして、敵のHPを一定値削ると、敵の攻撃パターンが変化していく。  ココの部分でもアクションゲーマー的には面白い仕様があり、このパターンの変化を「スキップ」させる事が可能なのだ。  先ほど言ったように、敵は大きなスキを見せる瞬間があるのだが、実は他のタイミングでも特定の技を活用したり、しっかりとプレイヤー側で対策を行えば攻撃を当てることが可能なのだ。  そのため、慣れてしまえば、大ダメージを与えて、敵の特定のパターン変化をスキップさせたり、あるいはそのまま撃破してしまう事も出来たりする。  もちろん、その場合は慣れに加えて事前の対策も必要となる。  例えば、本作では自キャラの能力を変化させる「ヒラメキ」と呼ばれる装備品（厳密には“装備”ではないが）もあり、こちらと組み合わせることで色々とプレイスタイルを変化させる事もできる。  こちらは「ホロウナイト」で言うところの「チャーム」のようなモノだと思ってくれればイイ。  これを活用して事前の準備、対策を行っていく形だ。  ちなみに、使用可能なアクション自体は探索を進める事で新たなものが解放されていくというメトロイドヴァニア系作品の基本を踏襲しているのだが、ある程度はボス戦を無視して、先に殆どのアクションを習得する事が出来る点も面白い仕様だ。  また、基本的に訪れたエリアで発生するボス戦は、そのエリアで入手できる能力を使用する事を前提に作られているのだが、事前に色々と能力を入手できるという仕組みを悪用して、例えば「二段ジャンプ」等を先に取っておくとちょっとした“ズル”ができたりする。  二段ジャンプ等のアクションと聞くと、プラットフォーマー面も気になると思うが、こちらに関して次の探索周りの項目でまとめて解説させてもらう。   [h1]探索とプラットフォーマー要素[/h1] ◆ 探索では攻略を通して入手できる能力、ダッシュ、二段ジャンプ、下段突きによる「ポゴ」、壁登り等の移動能力を活用して行っていく。  先ほど述べたように、これらの殆どの能力は各エリアの大ボスとの戦闘を無視して先に入手する事が可能であるため、先に入手し、探索を進行させる事も可能だ。  メインを進める分にはこれらの能力の「チュートリアル」となるようなプラットフォームエリアが登場する程度となるが、自身のHP、インク、装備できるヒラメキの量を上昇させるなどの重要のアイテムが配置、隠されている場所の大半は難易度が高かったり、それぞれのアクションの応用、プレイ面での工夫が必要となるような、所謂「アスレチック」系のエリアの攻略を楽しめる。  もちろん、シンプルに壊れる壁の向こう側に配置されていたりもする。 壊れる壁自体は、「ひびが入った」かのような表現がなされているので、そこらじゅうの壁を親の仇のように叩きまわる必要は無いので安心して欲しい。  また、メトロイドヴァニア系作品では特定の能力を持っていないために通れない場所や、特定のパズルが攻略できないといった事が発生するが、本作ではボタン一つでゲーム内要素として「スクリーンショット（ゲーム内名称は別）」を撮ることができ、メニュー内のマップからいつでもその場所を確認することができる機能を搭載している。  この影響によって、序盤で通れなかった場所のスクショさえ撮っておけば、後で「あれは何処だっけ？」となる心配がなくなる。  こちらの機能自体は本作独自のモノではなく、現代のメトロイドヴァニア系作品のいくつかで採用されているモノなのだが、非常に便利なので、メトロイドヴァニア系作品の開発者様には全ての作品で採用して欲しいところだ。  このような便利な機能こそあるが、実は本作の数少ない欠点も探索面にある。  まず、マップ内の各エリアにはエレベーターのようなモノが配置されており、それを使用してファストトラベルができるのだが、場所が決まっているため、そこから離れた場所の探索を行う場合は少し面倒だ。  ただし、本作はマップがそれほど広いわけではないので、ストレスを感じる事は少ないはずだ。  個人的に気にプレイしていて気になった事としては、メニューからマップを確認する時に「ズームアウト」できない事だ。  「ズームアウト」する項目自体はあるのだが、これを行うと周りの部屋、ルームが見える通常のマップではなく、絵のようなマップが表示されてしまうのだ。 各ルームの繋がりを把握したい場合はメニュー内で「移動」を行うほかない。  このように、気になる点も探索周りに集中しているが、それも殆ど些細なモノだ。  先ほど言ったように、本作のマップは同ジャンルの作品と比べて小さめとなっているのだが、その分内容が濃いモノとなっており、完成度が高い。   [h1][b]----------------------【良い点】----------------------[/b][/h1]  + 物語、レベルデザイン、敵のデザインにしっかりとテーマが反映されている。  + 戦闘とアクション周りの完成度が高い。   + 探索面で現代的な要素を取り入れている。  + 短くも全体的に濃い内容。   [h1][b]----------------------【悪い点】----------------------[/b][/h1] - マップをズームイン/アウトすることができない。  - ファストトラベルの位置が固定＆少ない。  - ボリュームはやや少なめ。   [h1][b]-----------------------【総評】----------------------[/b][/h1] 本作は、主人公の内面世界とゲーム体験を強く結びつけた作品だと言える。  精神的なテーマを、ストーリーだけでなく、敵のデザイン、ステージ構成、色使い、ボス戦の演出にまで落とし込んでいる点が非常に印象的だ。  アクション面では「ホロウナイト」からの影響を感じさせつつも、独自のリソース管理要素によって、プレイヤーに判断を迫り、ボス戦での駆け引きをより深いものにしている。  探索面でも、自由度が高く、「本来想定されている順序」を崩すことで楽ができたり、逆に高難易度の探索に挑めたりと、完成度が高い。  現代的で便利な仕組みも取り入れられており、探索そのものが快適だ。  細かな不満点はあるものの、それらは致命的な欠点ではなく、全体としての完成度の高さを揺るがすものでも無い。  メトロイドヴァニア系が好きな人はもちろん、短くも濃いアクション作品を楽しみたい方、物語面で「感情や内面を描く表現」に興味がある方などに、強くオススメできる作品となっている。  [hr][/hr] [h1]Falls Short of a Truly Joyful Experience[/h1]  I feel bad giving Constance a thumbs down – it’s harsh. But the truth is that I forced myself to finish it (with a meagre 88% completion rate) rather than doing so because I was having a lot of fun. The protagonist is beautifully animated, the controls are precise, and the abilities – all fashioned around the idea of color and brush being the “weapon” – work well. The meta story is presented in nicely done cutscenes or mini-games. Yet it all doesn’t come together as a coherent, atmospheric, or gripping experience.  Without going into details, the game visualises a fight against inner demons. But neither the scenery nor the common enemies’ designs seem to fit this theme; instead, they feel arbitrary and generic. For example, why robots?  With great gameplay and boss fights, satisfying exploration and platforming, such flaws can be overlooked. Unfortunately, Constance tends to impose what I’d call pedantic difficulty. While the standard platforming and fights are not very hard, the game has no tolerance for small mistakes. You can’t excel at one moment to compensate for another; each input has to be precise.  The optional platforming sections for extra loot and achievements become increasingly unforgiving, to the point where you have to execute about 50 different moves (I may be exaggerating) without making a single mistake — and without any respawn points in between. This is where the game lost me, as it is labeled a Metroidvania and not a precision platformer.  Especially the parts where you have to repeatedly press different buttons very quickly (and I mean very quickly), one after another to avoid hitting the ceiling, were too much for my aging reflexes. With enough practice and patience, I probably could have managed them (I did platinum MIO), but as the game failed to instill that hunger or make it rewarding enough, I gave up and instead finished the story as quickly as possible.  Instead of including accessibility options to negate different kinds of damage, I think it’s better game design to allow a bit of wiggle room, different approaches to a problem, and more freedom in combining abilities. (Both Ori games are good examples of doing just that.) But Constance also makes a mistake many Metroidvanias make, in my opinion: restricting movement for too long and giving you movement abilities too late in the game.  Combining double jump, dash, grapple (and pogo) allows for so much more freedom of movement — and thus more joy in exploration — that it seems like a poor decision to give you double jump as the last ability at about 80% game completion. (Maybe that was just me and I could have gotten it earlier - yet a lot of stuff is almost impossible to find if you're not relying on a guide - which you really shoudn't have to do in a well-designed game.) In combination with a horrible map, much too limited fast travel options, and the unforgiving platforming, exploration is discouraged rather than encouraged.  In the end, I simply didn’t want to revisit levels for exploration because it felt too tedious and complicated. What use is a map where each room is just a color blob without any hint of the layout? Taking the screenshot ability from Prince of Persia is nice, but let me pin the screenshot to a location on the map — don’t just auto-add it at the bottom of a room (the rooms can be pretty large).  Finally, the boss fights are mostly pure pattern learning. Again, be patient and execute everything exactly as intended, and you’ll succeed. Memory is a bigger requirement than skill.  Despite some nice ideas and competent execution, Constance left me more exhausted than exhilarated — and that’s not what I’m looking for in a Metroidvania.  5/10

Constance is a rather nice little Metroidvania with very sophisticated mechanics and controls. You have to get good at them - otherwise, you will fail, and not gently!   The art style is beautiful and meticulously crafted, but be warned: some sections are really tough. Is it Silksong-hard? No, because this is a completely different game.  It’s closer to a precision platformer, where mastering combinations of multiple moves is essential to make progress. In that sense, it feels more in the vein of Guacamelee than a traditional Souls-leaning metroidvania.  Bosses feel original and challenging, the combat is very well thought of and feels on point. If you enjoy tight controls, demanding execution, and learning through failure, this game will reward you.  Is it hard overall? Definitely. Some sections are not optional and exceptionally demanding - IMHO, they even rival the toughest platforming challenges out there.

The game asks more of you than it'll ever give you.  The artwork and animation is beautiful, I'm not certain how much of it is actually "Hand-drawn" since hand-animating 60 fps and beyond isn't really feasible, but the presentation is lovely.  The game kind of lacks substance though, in that it gets monotonous very quickly, and the scaling difficulty in both the enemies and platforming is definitely the opposite of de-stressing. This is a part of the Metroidvania niche that values Soulslike difficulty over constancy and substance. If that's your thing, then all the power to you.  It feels like the vision was Moonscars, but with the mechanics of Epic Mickey.  I was too late to get a refund, though if anyone is interested in the product I would definitely suggest waiting until it's on sale.

First let me say.. Even though I recommend this game, I definitely have mixed opinions due to playing other platformer games [b](Not saying this is a bad one though, it definitely brings a challenge at times!)[/b] And additionally, there'll be SLIGHT spoilers, with one decently big spoiler but I'll be using [spoiler]This tag[/spoiler] to cover them up, hopefully that worked. They won't be game-ending spoilers if you want to read it, it'll mostly just be things you'll find mid-game! If the spoilers don't work, I'll uhh delete my review and fix it. If it matters to you, I only have ~80% of the game completed as of writing this.  I really liked the game at the start, it felt pretty simple to understand and defeating enemies felt easy.. For a bit, sometime after there was a normal enemy with a very large attack, in which I had no idea how to normally avoid. There doesn't seem to be anything to do but tank the damage if you [spoiler]don't have dash yet[/spoiler], even then it's hard to even get damage in with that. Some more enemies I had issues with was uh, [spoiler]that DAMN circus with the first boss cards and second boss clown.[/spoiler]. The first one's attack patterns take a while to get used to, sometimes it's not clear what to do or makes you ask if you have everything you even need for this boss. The second one's patterns are very simple, but you have NO idea what to do at first.. I though that one attack went on endlessly until I figured out you can, jump on those things???? And you HAVE to. It REALLY doesn't help when those bosses, [spoiler]have to be run back AGAIN FOR SOME REASON!!! The first Cards boss, but WAY stronger, and the second Clown boss, but WAY more annoying[/spoiler]. The first one is REALLY unclear of where they'll attack even after attempting to fight them a few more times, it feels draining to deal with. The second one was just created to annoy the hell out of you even if it's the same exact strategy to deal with them, not fun and I don't see why this had to be done..  The platforming is great, I personally didn't have an issue with it! Though a few broken walls feel a bit.. BS. They never feel a bit obvious and I have to re-visit rooms several times to find one, in which is only a small reward at best. It shows you a breakable wall near the start so you go "Oh cool, I better be on the lookout for those" and I don't think it shows you at least two for a LONG while (I could be misremembering). [b](Now actually a big spoiler for, I'd say around mid-game)[/b] [spoiler]The Chase scene was kind of weird for me. I knew that when I died, it wasn't entirely the game's fault but it wasn't partly mine either.. You felt WAY too rushed at some parts, you'd have to remember so much to get past it in a few lives that any other boss felt forgivable. It was really annoying to deal with, and it just never comes up again?? That's WEIRD to me, never bringing it up ever again feels so wrong but whatever..[/spoiler].  Then there comes the end, honestly the game ended relatively fast for a metroidvania.. It's not bad, for sure, but it felt sudden I guess? A bit too fast when you approached the end, but that's not my issue! No big spoilers don't worry, just two important game-mechanic throughout the whole game: [spoiler]Heart pieces, and Erasers.[/spoiler] These two items are going to be VERY important for your runs, but you don't NEED them. Hell, the issue I had with them is that I only got around 4-5 [spoiler]Heart pieces[/spoiler] and 2 [spoiler]Erasers[/spoiler] by the time the final boss was defeated. As a reminder, [spoiler]Erasers are important as they let you equip more Inspirations. As far as I know, there's 5 of them?[/spoiler] One was guaranteed I think, and one was obtained from the shop.. I can not tell you if the others require super-hard parkour or are behind broken walls, considering I didn't find any of that from my playthrough. I wish I could've found more as my ass was kinda kicked by the final boss, even though I eventually got through it with learning attack patterns.  The controls/attacks are neat, and one of them even has an additional way to activate it through keybinds or an attack: [spoiler]The pogo jump!!![/spoiler] The story was pretty consistent and I generally understood what it was about, though the start has me very confused on how [spoiler]we even ended up here, maybe I missed something..? Is this another world, or just a dream??[/spoiler]. Overall, great game but could've probably used a bit more time in making it longer (Personally, I just like longer games), and some more balance tweaks along the way PLEASE.

Review honesta de este juego, aquí va. Para empezar, si esperan que este sea un juego "chill" o para relajarse, no lo van a encontrar; artísticamente es un juego precioso, animaciones fluidas, personajes únicos y con un buen soundtrack, por otra parte, la jugabilidad en ciertos aspectos se siente horrible; que dos de tus ataques más importantes en un juego (ataque hacia arriba y hacia abajo) requieran pintura resulta muy molesto cuando necesitas usarlos además de necesitar usar otras de tus habilidades que también gastan pintura, entiendo que está hecho a posta, pero no le quita lo molesto, por otra parte que el mapa no se desactive presionando el TAB, sino que tienes que darle dos veces para que se quite, también resulta molesto cuando sin querer le das al botón en media pelea y de repente tienes el mapa estorbándote la pantalla. Además, siento que este juego no tiene ese paso de fácil/principiante - a difícil/estado avanzado; sino que desde un inicio los challenges son complicados, nuevamente, es un juego metroidvania de plataformas así que es esperable que estén así, pero desde el principio en el que no tienes ninguna habilidad es un poco cruel. Habiendo jugado otros juegos de su género (Hollow Knight, Silksong, Ori and the blind forest/will of the wisps...) aún así se me complicó bastante. Por el apartado histórico, me parece hermoso, si eres artista como yo te vas a ver reflejado en varios aspectos del juego, realmente te hace auto reflexionar sobre algunas cosas y cómo nosotros mismos nos convertimos en nuestro peor enemigo, siento que el juego transmite esta metáfora de una forma muy bien lograda. Realmente quisiera ver qué más logran hacer con este juego en un futuro! En resumen; si eres fan de los juegos que mencioné anteriormente, seguramente te vaya a gustar, sin embargo no esperen un nivel de dificultad fácil solo por ser un juego con estética cozy, yo por lo personal lo recomiendo :).

[h1]Constance[/h1] [h2]✅ Recommandé — [b]MajiNote : B[/b][/h2]  [hr][/hr]  [h2]📌 Contexte[/h2] [list] [*][b]Temps de jeu :[/b] 7h30 [*][b]Mode conseillé :[/b] Solo [*][b]Avis :[/b] J’ai présenté/testé la démo, puis fait le jeu à sa sortie. C’est beau, c’est sincère, mais… [/list]  [h2]📜 Lecture du Culte[/h2] Constance est un metroidvania 2D dessiné à la main, centré sur une artiste et un monde intérieur en déclin, avec un thème “burnout / santé mentale”. Développé par :contentReference[oaicite:0]{index=0}, publié par :contentReference[oaicite:1]{index=1} (et :contentReference[oaicite:2]{index=2} selon les régions), sorti sur :contentReference[oaicite:3]{index=3} le 24 novembre 2025.  [h2]⚙️ La Boucle[/h2] Tu explores des zones, tu récupères des upgrades, tu reviens ouvrir des chemins, et tu progresses sur une structure metroidvania assez classique. La “signature”, c’est que ton kit tourne autour de la peinture : une ressource qui sert à des actions / attaques / mouvements, avec une logique de gestion simple mais efficace. Et oui : il y a des passages de plateforme pure qui montent bien en pression et qui deviennent vraiment captivants.  [h2]✅ Ce que le Culte approuve[/h2] [list] [*][b]DA dessinée à la main :[/b] c’est beau, attachant, ça a une vraie patte. [*][b]Le sujet :[/b] c’est pas “juste un skin”, il y a une vraie intention autour de la santé mentale / l’épuisement, et ça mérite d’être mis en avant. [*][b]Plateforme par moments vraiment solide :[/b] quand le jeu te demande d’être propre, il est très bon (et c’est ce que j’ai préféré). [*][b]Le jeu est propre :[/b] ça se joue bien, ça se lit bien. [/list]  [h2]⚠️ Ce que le Culte déplore[/h2] [list] [*][b]Metroidvania assez “sage” :[/b] au-delà de sa DA et de son thème, la formule reste classique. [*][b]Court pour le genre :[/b] 7–8h, c’est vite plié si tu traces. [*][b]Surcoté par une partie de la presse :[/b] je comprends les notes (beau, thème fort, quelques très bons passages), mais ça ne mérite pas “le nouveau messie du metroidvania”. [*][b]Il ne va pas au bout de son potentiel :[/b] il aurait pu pousser plus loin l’ambition (variété, surprises, densité). [/list]  [h2]🩸 Moment Culte[/h2] Les cut-scenes émouvantes qui te prennent aux tripes. C’est vraiment quelque chose de marquant dans ce jeu.  [h2]🎯 Recommandé si…[/h2] Tu veux un metroidvania beau, accessible, avec un thème rare et des passages de plateforme vraiment cool, et que t’es ok avec une expérience courte et assez classique.  [hr][/hr]  [quote=Maji / Le Culte] [b]Conclusion :[/b] Je mets B : c’est un bon jeu, avec une DA attachante et un vrai sujet. Mais ça reste trop “sage” et trop court pour que je crie au banger comme certains. [b]Le Culte valide : B.[/b] [/quote]  [i]Rejoins le groupe Steam du Culte de Maji : [url=https://steamcommunity.com/groups/majitv]Le Culte de Maji[/url][/i]

I really adore this game! it's the first metroidvania I've played since silksong but I wouldn't compare the two - to me this game reminded me much more of celeste. it's not easy but it won't rob you and wants you to win, the movement feels great and the platforming is satisfying but forgiving, the art and animations are stylish and lovely, the core of the story about mental health is very realistic and honest without overstaying it's welcome. the bosses especially made the whole game a blast for me once my brain was out of silksong soulslike mode being mostly platforming in fun creative ways, and tho the base combat isn't anything too special the game is short enough that I only felt I was mastering it by the end. the main story took me 9.5 hours and anything after is returning for quests, and achievements. it's certainly not without flaws but none would stop me from recommending and they're already committing to map and boss patches which is really cool. I came to Constance from silksong (my first metroidvania) and thought the gameplay would be similar and I was VERY wrong. If you're a big fan of hollow knight and think the combat is gonna be that challenging then I don't recommend it. The game is mostly about platforming then it's combat in my opinion and that isn't bad. The combat isn't as complex as silksong because of it's simple enemies, but the platforming sections for the optional abilities were really fun and some of them were worth the challenging platforming.  There is a health bar for bosses which is really nice but, I liked not stressing about how close I was to killing a boss so I turned it off at the start. The bosses are hard but not silksong hard I would say, The final boss of the game was the most interesting because of it's different movesets, but got really easy to master after a while and I mostly was trying to perfect the parkour part of the boss. saying this most bosses took me 30m-1hr which is the same for silksong but I'm 36 hours into silksong and only 50% through the game unlike constance which I finished in around 24hrs, constance doesn't have enough bosses so I wish there was a harder mode to compensate for the low amount of bosses.   There is nothing else I wanna say but if you're looking for hardcore combat right now then that's not what you're gonna get from constance, they did say they're gonna add a map overhaul, new gamemodes and improved boss fights so I will be changing my review when they add these things because the game art style really compels me. again If you do care about fun platforming then this is a good for you.

7到8分  抛开开头的教程地图，游戏能探索的地方只有5个大型区域，在类银河城游戏里偏少，导致了流程较短。玩家没有提升攻击力的手段，收集品大部分为新的能力和动作，因此BOSS战大部分都是机制BOSS，需要玩家使用最近获得的几种能力，专注躲避一段时间后才露出一个输出窗口。游戏没有难度选项，因此在某个BOSS被卡住就只能一直练躲避技巧直到通过，可能存在卡关状况。 只玩主线的话，打通关都用不到二段跳，跑图有难度的地方都放置在支线收集。收集过程中有几张横跨两到三个屏幕的跳跳乐地图，中间没有落脚点，且需要交替使用下劈、突刺、冲刺等动作，难度比较大，好在操作手感不错，动作流畅，算一个加分项。 地图的存档点比较少，椅子不能快速移动，每张地图只有一个电梯可以传送，查漏补缺时有些难受。由于椅子很少，游戏还提供了一个免除跑路过程的选项，代价是敌人的攻击力提高。这个设计在开荒时非常方便，为了节省重复跑图时间，一般情况我都会选这个选项，相当于加了一点点游戏难度，但是死亡的代价变得几乎没有。 小地图是一个个方块拼接而成，不显示地图细节，但是会显示每个房间是否存在电梯、人物或存档点。探索完成的房间会有颜色稍淡点的斜线覆盖，本身颜色就不明显，游戏也没做任何提示，直到最后找剩下的2%内容时我才发现。游戏用一个拍照功能代替了标点，玩下来的感受是并不好用，时间久了回来看照片也不知道是哪个位置，回到对应房间后还是需要不停查看照片确定标记的位置，唯一的优点是和一个需要拍照的支线任务结合的比较巧妙。  整体看来亮点是操作手感和美术风格，剧情简短且都是通过鼠标点击和拖动的动作展现，代入感尚可，可以一玩。

If you are like me, Constance is a great short journey to fuel the hollowness created by hollow knight series with its fast-pace parkouring, combat and satisfying art-style in its painted world adorned by unique color and emotion for every level. It has a parallel storytelling appearing along the usual hardcore metroidvania experience and between main bosses to reveal the protagonist's motivation. All its soundtracks, level design and puzzle / skill introductions are well combined and easily pique your interest. Also has the most navigational helpful feature ever that all games with complex map / level layout.  [hr][/hr] Since I am so close ending it I cannot conclude whether game will really leave a great taste at the end but I can share my favorite features that keep fueling my hype throughout the game:  [hr][/hr]  + Astonishing art-style & OSTs with heartwarming melodies. The style is very cute and very well-drawn in cartoon, reminding me Cuphead but more livelier and also combining contrast and opposite themes as in colors and emotions throughout the story. And each melodies through the journey feels very consistent showing how passionately created this world. Last time I felt lost in the main menu listening the theme track was the Dark Souls 3 and now I have found a new game that touch my heart with each piano tile hitting the string.  + Capturing photo, a mechanic that introduced for a side quest, but also mainly to keep tracking hidden areas or areas that you will return later when you got the specific skills to complete there. You have limited films for your camera and each photo you take stored in your map (which can be also increased by finding more or by purchasing), helping you mark your map and navigate more easily. This feature alone what makes the navigation between levels more endurable. You can take a break from this game for days or months, then return it and remember what you were doing or where was the hidden rooms that was not accessible before, easily.  + One main combat mechanic is that there is a unique 'Dye bar' (purple dye that gives the protag its color). It works like stamina mechanic, which is used by all skills that paint the world while activating and balance spamming. When its all out, protag enters a 'inkless' state (becomes literally Sam Manson from Danny Phantom cartoon series) and trying to use a skill spending dye result as successfully applying skill but harm yourself, depleting a part of the HP bar (white bar).  + Another mechanic is about choices represented when you die. You can start at last place you died, but apply a 'Puppet Curse', which makes every enemy stronger but also drop more material + small health. Other choice is classic reviving at last shrine you meditated without any consequences. Personally using curse double the enjoyment of the boss fights (yea bosses are also affected :3 ) and many 'Path of Endurance' parts for hidden rooms.  + Lastly the skill management system, called "Inspirations", is not only limits you according to your unlocked skill slots but also how do you organize them with their Tetris-like shapes in your page. Most of the inspirations also affect more or less according to the adjacent shapes. Getting new inspirations are also very fun and satisfactory to watch. You discover new inspiration by following secret paths in most levels, leading protag to an area to get inspired by natural scenery and noting them.

Constance is a mix of mechanics I would love to see in other metroidvanias and issues that just make me not wanna play it anymore.  Firstly, the game is undercooked. It needs more polish and optimization. There are entire rooms that are made unplayable (<20 fps) just because there are 5 png's rotating. Combat seems clunky with some very weird hitboxes, specially the card deck bossfight.  Some good aspects are that movement feels very nice, except for the dash. This ability for some reason consumes ink (mana) and when you run out you TAKE DAMAGE. I have never seen a game mixing magic and movement resources and then actively punish me when I inevitably run out of ink. It happens often during parkour sections when I would take almost guaranteed damage. To add insult to injury, devs are completely aware, with an upgrade that removes that issue but you can't equip right away as it is too big to fit.

Constance just barely gets a recommendation from me, consider it a short metroidvania with a lot of flaws and get it on a steep discount.  The good Art is awesome, boss fights are a highlight and different from what you expect from similar games. Most boss fights have very aggressive phases that are long were you need to just survive and then a clear opening to strike the boss for a few seconds when he is standing still doing nothing. It was hard for me in the beginning because if you try to damage a boss during an agro phase you are just going to die, fast.  The bad Mapdesign is quite weak and exploring isn't that fun, I never felt compelled to backtrack to old locations to see what was behind that door than you couldn't unlock previously. The game incorporates a lot of platforming and special challenges just to progress through rooms which makes backtracking tedious and unrewarding.  Narrative for me was atrocious, its a game about stress, its going to try and make you stressed, make you feel bad and hammer home the message that life never gets better. There's no light here and while the game didn't manage to make me connect with the narrative it still made me feel bad, which might be good for some people. But for me making me feel bad gets minus points for me because it makes me enjoy my time with the game less.  The platforming, hard in all the wrong ways. I generally don't connect well with precision platformers, Celeste and I have a bit of a love/hate relationship (great but too hard for me). If you are a person that likes precision platforming you will probably enjoy your time here more than me. For the rest of you just be warned, there is a long platforming sequence (timed) in the game that is very hard in frustrating ways. A lot of the time it throws things at you so fast that you don't have time to see what to do, and therefore you die. Its designed around the fact that you have to fail, several times just to learn what to do. It turns into a kind of QTE where you know what you need to click in what order but the execution is tricky.  Another point about the platforming, this games combines at least four different buttons (abilities) that you need to combine in the right order during platforming fast. Maybe that would work for a long game, were you get really used to the skills but in a 7-8 hour long game? For me it was frustrating juggling 3-4 different buttons and timings in the air.

I'm on the last few bosses of this and I'm not sure if I'll finish it. Largely good game. Love that it has snapshot mechanics. I love the art style and it has quite a lot of personality in its metroidvania gameplay elements. I particularly like the Lord Koba boss and how it utilises that super fun metal box mechanic, however I could really do without that one part in that area involving that mechanic where you have to get lucky with the timing of a laser guy while being chased by 2 phones and hence unable to stop. Unless there's something you can do to deal with those phones, that part is essentially luck-based and it took me more than half an hour, it was infuriating.  That one specific part aside, I do have a couple of general issues with the game too. The first is a thematic one, that being that the connection between the gameplay or even the genre of gameplay and the themes, particularly the cutscenes, is very loose; this is a mostly minor complaint but if the creators really wanted to create an evocative outlet for these themes then a metroidvania isn't really going to work. The other grievance I had was with the health bar system; health upgrades are ridiculously sparse, and while you can unlock a small health regen if you find more life pots while on full health, you don't respawn with that regen available. So bossfights are scaled in difficulty to quite a substantial degree depending on whether there's an easily accessible health pot next to the nearest spawn point, which I found pretty irritating and is a large part of the reason I've stopped the game after a few attempts at the Foundry Citadel bossfight which killed me stupidly quickly.   I'd still recommend this but it'll help to be better at bossfights than I am, because if you need that health regen then some of the bosses are gonna be ragequit-level demotivating.

I had a complicated relationship with this game, so I will list the pros and cons I had, so people can be informed if it will be a good fit for them :).  TLDR: I'd give it a 6.8/10, it's not for everyone, and I know some people will enjoy it more than me. The art style if beautiful and I don't regret playing at all, just not the best fit for me from a gameplay aspect!  Pros:  -Great art -good soundtrack - fun and rewarding exploration - unique upgrade system for abilities  Cons:  - Boss bottles are not engaging (to me) they are a lot of dodge set attack pattern followed by damage phase (I prefer more dynamic / engaging battles where you can attack while bosses attack). I would say if boss bottles are your favorite part of metroidvanias, this isn't the best, though the final boss was fun (but not very challenging) - Platforming is overall good, however, I found the control unintuitive due to having to use basically every phase button for a different movement, a minor complaint, but something I found frustrating at times

It's a fun game heavily inspired by Hollow Knight. Standard HK-style combat, some good platforming sections, bosses were ok but a little easy imo.  Unfortunately it suffers from being way too short. It feels like they intended for it to be 3 times the size, then decided to wrap it up with only 5 full zones because it was taking too long. Kind of reminds me Gestalt: Steam & Cinder in that respect.  Storyline is vaguely modern about burnout or depression or something, kind of in the same vein as Celeste, but unfortunately it didn't hit the right notes for me. I'm glad they minimized the story because I wasn't really feeling it. Which is fine for a short metroidvania honestly.  Level design was nice, I would have liked to see the creativity taken up just one notch but it was pretty good. Platforming sections were probably the most challenging aspect of the game, but I could have appreciated some sections that were even harder.  All in all took about 13 hours for 100%. I'd like for them to make a sequel because I think they're onto something. If they can double/triple the length, make harder bosses, and take the creativity up a little bit on the level design/platforming I think a sequel could be really great.

So people are saying how this game has unsatisfying combat, boring bosses, and unreasonably challenging platforming like these are all bad things. But really, this is the only metroidvania I've played that felt actually fun rather than frustrating.  For example, consider combat, bosses in particular. None of the bosses test how well you can evade their attacks while trying to get close enough often enough to defeat them — something I'm very bad at and hate with a passion (not sure which caused what honestly). Like, where in other games you usually have to hit the boss enough times to stun it, in Constance it presents itself to your "mercy" if you wait long enough (without dying in the process), perhaps after doing some puzzle-solving. So if you're patent and avoid taking damage, you are basically guaranteed to defeat it. If you're not... well, runbacks are quite short — you'll manage eventually.  Now, platforming. The "main route" involves almost no difficult platforming, but there are some fairly hard optional sections. And the sidescrolling parts, I guess. But I've played Celeste, and once you've beaten ch9... Suffice to say, Constance's challenges were pretty tame in comparison. I think the only difficult part was making sure I used the right abilities, of which there were too many. Like, if you're making a game and run out of the W/A/S/D/Q/E keys, I think you should stop and think before bringing in another ability and binding it to V — utterly insane given how often you need it (rebound it to W — [i]much[/i] nicer this way). I guess it'll make you a better piano player at least?  And other stuff. The visuals are pretty; animations are nice and smooth. The story... meh; didn't resonate with me at all. Not that I cared anyway. NPCs feel anemic and robotic. The music is alright. Also, couldn't pan the map in the menu for some reason.  To conclude, I'd suggest viewing this as platforming with a sprinkle of combat — that's what it felt like to me.

It's a really nice game, and visually very well executed. but unfortunately there are many design choices that are just stupid. Platforming sections that demand memorizing long stretches of level design and executing with perfect timing or else you need to do it all over again from the beginning.  Other times, puzzles will require you to activate switches while platforming above spikes in precise time intervals that you can only discover by pure trial and error. There are also many screens that were built as a small platforming challenge to get through, but once you are exploring for other reasons, they are just impossible to backtrack through.  It's quite ironic that the game is about a main character going through burnout, and then the game designers go and make the difficulty spike absurdly for absolutely no reason. Are they trying to give us burnout?  If I wanted that type of challenge I'd be playing Silksong

El juego vale mucho la pena y es divertido de jugar. Su jugabilidad es muy cómoda, fluida e intuitiva. Visualmente es muy lindo; diría que ese es su punto más fuerte, seguido por la jugabilidad. La historia está bien: es simple y complementaria.  Ahora bien, también tiene sus cosas malas (esto es solo mi opinión). Entre ellas está su dificultad reducida, tanto en enemigos como en jefes. Siendo más concreto, hay pocos enemigos en cada zona y suelen sentirse más como decoración. Los jefes, a pesar de ser bastante originales, caen en lo mismo: son muy simples.  Por último, está el precio. A pesar de que lo compré en oferta, me parece un poco caro teniendo en cuenta que hay otros juegos del mismo género que pueden ofrecer lo mismo o más a un mejor precio base.  Pero todo esto no lo convierte en un mal juego; son solo detalles que, a mi criterio, le restan puntos.  Dicho todo esto, mi nota para Constance es un 7.5. Vale la pena, así que pruébenlo si buscan una experiencia tranquila. A mí me tomó 34 horitas conseguir todos sus logros y fue muy disfrutable.

I enjoyed the game, the devs clearly love the genre and had an interesting take on it (HollowCelestetroidvania).   Good:   The art is beautiful, the music is great and the story is definitely something a lot of creatives struggle with. Gameplay is 98% solid and most of the skills are introduced well so you can build on them as the game progresses.   Bad: Literally just one thing and two words.            The thing: A chase sequence that has a massive change in difficulty that almost feels like ragebait in  comparison to the rest of the game. If you're decent at platforming games, you can beat it, but right now it  feels either intentionally aggravating or really unbalanced.            The two words: Spike tunnels. These feel like they were designed for exactly one controller style, and  everyone outside of that tax bracket is screwed.  Needs work (as of 01/26):   As me and some other player have noted in the discussions, some mechanics could use rebalancing. Both of the "bad" things could be rebalanced to keep you involved in the experience, rather than pulling you out. One of the skills (plunge) is designed for button use, but also accepts the classic combo "joystick down + attack". If you're wired to use the classic combo and miss that plunge is intended for button use, it can make some sections (spike tunnels, skill challenges) overly difficult. None of the problems are really game-breaking, but they could use some revision. Right now, it's like if you were watching an art film and out of nowhere there's just bloody footage from a grindhouse director and then poof, back to the art film.   Depending on how familiar you are with the genre will dictate how much playtime you get (I got most of the  achievement in the first run in about 12 hours, and there's speedrun achievements).   Overall it's a good game and I hope the devs feel proud of all the work they've put into it, because it does show.

This is not necessarily a bad game, but it isn't one I would recommend. Despite the amount of work and heart the devs have put into the game, it just doesn't quite hold up against similar games in its genre.  At the heart of the gameplay is an identity issue that leads to both the combat and the platforming feeling underdeveloped. I wholeheartedly think the game could have worked much better if it'd stuck to [i]just[/i] platforming, and maybe could've worked just as well if it had focused on combat. But in it's final form, enemies only have 1 attack, while bosses have you dodging until the phase where you can deal damage. Similarly, platforming just isn't refined enough and just too infrequent to feel like a core part of the game, leading to platforming sections feeling frustrating despite being fairly easy, as the bulk of the game has you accustomed to no platforming.  Unfortunately the game world is also lackluster, missing rewarding exploration along with any form of lore whatsoever (although the lack of lore isn't a huge deal IMO). The backtracking element that you'd expect to find here doesn't really exist. Each area is practically done once you get the Thingamajig™ and the only backtracking is to get the few health or paint upgrades that lie scattered about. Skills felt unimpactful and forgettable and don't change how the game plays.  Despite all that I am thoroughly impressed with the animation, which is fluid and very good. It clearly shows that the devs had fun making the game. I'll leave out my thoughts on the little story there is as I'm not the right audience for it, and it'd probably hit harder to someone other than me. The game is also somewhat short, but that doesn't really impact my thoughts on it as a whole  It feels wrong to leave a "negative" review despite the devs passion, but my time playing left me wanting to switch to different, better executed games. Again, this isn't strictly a bad game and some of the platforming, along with the final boss, were quite fun. But the rest of the game felt vapid and unrefined, and unfortunately because of that I don't feel I can recommend this when it stands in the company of many other similar games.

Su música acompaña de manera sutil y profunda, reforzando los estados de ánimo del juego y creando una atmósfera envolvente que conecta directamente con el jugador. Más allá de lo visual y sonoro, sobresale por su mensaje honesto y empático sobre la sobrecarga mental, abordando este tema con respeto y simbolismo, sin caer en excesos ni superficialidad. Сюжет в игре подаётся плохо. Поскольку диалогов мало разработчики пытались падать его через окружения что вышло как по мне плохо. Как я понял эта история о творческом кризисе художницы и её депрессии.  Визуальный стиль игры потрясный только из-за него после демки я купил игру. Ручная рисовка сразу бросается в глаза. Цветовая палитра меняется от мрачных и тревожных тонов до ярких когда главная героиня использует свои силы. Анимации так же сделаны неплохо. Движения выглядят плавно и это радует.  Музыка в игре не запоминающаяся и ощущается вся одинаково.  Основная фишка игры это превращение себя в краску. Благодаря этому открывается много возможностей для платформинга которые разработчики слабо реализовали.  Карта...ну сделана нормально. Комнаты относительно небольшие так что нормально. Но будь она более подробной было бы лучше. Бэктрекинг в игре тоже не сказаться что интересный та и перемещение лифтом мне не очень понравилось. Лучше бы просто добавили возможно перемещения между кострами.  У боссам такая же претензия. Мне запомнилось только два босса и исключительно потому что они плохие. Все остальные просто проходные и никак не запоминаются. Босс в библиотеке от которого надо было убегать. Хоть у меня ушло относительно не много времени но столько херни я давно не видел в играх. С первого раза его буквально не возможно пройти из-за того что уровень на заучивание. Поскольку камера постоянно будет позади героини ты не видишь что в переди а если камера тебя догнала считай уровень уже слит ибо под конец уровня босс резко набирает скорость. Постоянные подлянки в виде шипов и телепорты в которые надо под правильным углом прыгнуть. Одним словом душный долгий и абсолютно не интересный босс. И к нему на пару идёт финальный босс. Достаточно того что он просто слияние трёх боссов которые были перед ним с АБСОЛЮТНО теми же атаками. У него только одна новая атака и всё, остальное уже было в предыдущих. Я думал это очередной босс после которого уже будет финальный но каково было моё разочарование. Выглядит он плохо бой не интересный (это в целом можно сказать про всех босов). Единственный босс который хоть как был интересный является не обязательным и его можно легко пропустить.  Я думал закрыть игру на 100% но увы просто нет желания. Игра никакая мейби найдутся люди которым она нравится но увы я её советовать к прохождению не буду.

It was a pretty good game. Short and sweet!  [h2] the map [/h2] [h3] the good [/h3] One of the best parts of the game was the marker system of the map. In most metroidvania-type games, you encounter plenty of places you want to revisit later, but you don't have a good way to mark them. At best, you have an icon without a label that you can place; at worst, you have to use means outside of the game to keep track (pen and paper or paint.net or whatever else). In this game though, the main marker is a [b]photo!![/b] You actually have a screenshot showing where the marker is placed. That's a great way to remember why you marked that place!! Truly appreciated.  [h3] the bad [/h3] [strike]You know which room you are in on the map, but you never know where in the room you are, which makes traversal a bit tedious at times. Say there is a room with 2 exits on the same side and you are in front of one. Since your icon is not placed next to that exit, you have no way to tell at a glance which exit you are in front of. You have to either keep track mentally of which door you entered the room through and what you did in the room to judge whether you are in front of the exit you want or not. It would have been appreciated if the icon of your character would move to different positions in the room (it doesn't have to be 1 to 1, just a few set positions that help situate our character). [/strike] All my grievances with the map has been address in patch 1.1  [h2] the difficulty [/h2] [h3] the regular enemies [/h3] The regular enemies are all pretty easy. I think it's fair to say that they are not here to frustrate the player, but to be more of a mild challenge to keep things interesting when traversing an area.  [h3] the bosses [/h3] The bosses have (in my opinion) a well-balanced difficulty. I probably first-tried only a couple of them, but every other one gave me enough to chew on that I had to learn a little bit about what I needed to do and when. All bosses have well-telegraphed moves that leave no room to wonder what needs to be done. It's all about learning how to react to those cues without fumbling in the middle of it. It's basically a mix of reflexes and muscle memory. It required me a few tries on most of the bosses (some a little bit more than others). The hardest boss to me [spoiler] was not a traditional boss but more of a platforming challenge. It's the only boss that is like that, really. [edit] this is some amount of time after I finished the game, looks like the patch 1.1 significantly changed that fight so it should be much easier now. I'm not dedicated enough to restart the game to double check.[/spoiler]  [h3] the platforming [/h3] Pretty enjoyable platforming—nothing that felt really new for that type of game, but well executed. Where the platforming really shined was in some of the optional sections to get some of the optional stuff. Some of those places required you to press a series of buttons in the right order, and it was a bit challenging not to cross my wires doing it. Nothing that a bit of muscle memory didn't fix though.  [h2] Conclusion [/h2] I really enjoyed the game. I got 100% in all but 2 regions before reaching the point where the game would end. One because I didn't figure out how to get past a breakable wall that I saw, and the other because I didn't figure out where the items to complete a couple of optional quests were located, and I didn't feel like going through every single room multiple times to find them. So right before triggering the end, I checked a guide to finish those last few optional percentages real quick. I wouldn't have bothered doing that if I didn't really enjoy the game.

Constance is a tremendous effort in a game. It's one I think needs to be played by Metroidvania fans for its style, soundtrack, and unique story. Where it faltered in some of the bigger boss fights for me might thrive for others. Constance is absolutely stunning in its fluid animations.  The cast of characters are charming, and the way it weaves together the dark inner world and real-life consequences is something I will never forget. I truly believe the animation for this game is something you need to play to experience truthfully.  Thankfully, you have options for accessibility, so if you do not enjoy fighting and its difficulties, you have the chance to remove all damage. Considering I was pressed for time, I did do that for one of the boss fights; otherwise, I wouldn't be here writing the review.  Aside from my obvious gripe with boss fights, the map itself isn't as straightforward as I would have liked. I won't get into too much detail and share why now.  What's great with video games, unlike movies, TV shows, or albums, is that they can offer quality-of-life improvements over time, and from the post-launch roadmap, it looks like that is coming in early 2026. Here is a quote from that update:  “Besides obvious accessibility options like a better readable map and skippable cutscenes, we are also looking into our boss fight designs and will smoothen out some of the rougher edges in terms of timing, pacing, and balancing that we unfortunately didn't have the time to perfect before the 1.0 launch.”  The game sits at an impressive 1,700 positive reviews on Steam as of this writing, and from what I see in the update coming, I wasn't the only one with feedback on the map and boss fights, which, when addressed, will clearly make Constance a must-play game.

先說評分3/10，所以給到不推薦，如果是以故事與美術當作想買的人可以看一下這篇。 我本來對這遊戲的要求不會到很高，我會先特別注意每款遊戲主打的特點是什麼再作評價， 當然了主打 類銀河戰士惡魔城 的遊戲還是免不了跟其他同類型的去做對比，像戰鬥、跳跳樂...等等 但康斯坦絲這款是以故事與美術作為特點的遊戲 玩的過程我卻就像是在喝白開水，一開始雖然是熱的，但越來越冷直到遊戲結束都一樣， 問題出在整個冒險非常空泛缺乏感情的鋪成，很多事情發生的非常突然，每次打完大boss都會切回主角記憶， 但這就讓玩家沒有辦法好好體會到主角實際上的情感波動，會有一種奇怪的隔閡感。 再說說整個地圖的設計，非常隨便，跟空洞騎士沒買羽毛筆的地圖樣子差不多 再來是戰鬥的部分，處於一個你打你的我打我的狀態，交互感差勁，少了類銀那種 我使出我全部的能力終於把boss幹掉了 的感覺 這款戰鬥基本上就是平a穿插普攻，遇到黑色球球補一發法術，無腦到有點粗糙。 還有打boss還要解謎的問題，這方面就見仁見智了，有些我覺得不錯，有些廢到想笑，就不多做評論。 我就講到這裡，還想嘗試的人推薦等到半價再買吧，不然會挺虧的。

Если катко, то игра на 5/10. Я бы сказал, что проходняк, но в игру было в целом приятно играть.  Плюсы:   -Визуал  -Исследование локаций  Минусы:   -Боёвка  -Персонажи и Задания  -Сюжет  -Музыка  Общий визуал игры - очень хорош. Цвета и анимации прям сочные, такое ощущение, что играешь в красивый мультик. Локации красивые, исследовать их интересно. Также порадовало то, что их можно исследовать в относительно произвольном порядке. Сразу же вспоминается Найн Солс, где тебя буквально садят на реальсы, заставляя все локации чистить подряд (выбор между городом и руинами не в счёт, он там всего один). Плюс игра тебя побуждает в них возвращаться. Ты увидел какую-то нычку или предмет, который ты сейчас не можешь достать - сфотографируй это место, потом вернись и доисследуй. Правда я не особо понимаю, а зачем было ограничивать фотографии, ибо какой-то прям нужны в них нет, можно было бы сделать их безлимитными.  Головоломок как таковых нет, есть только джамп пазлы и сегменты на время, где тебе надо быстро активировать пару каких-нибудь "рычагов".  Отдельно запомнились моменты с минутным джамп пазлом (который выбешивает похлеще любого уровня в селесте или того же пути боли), а также дверью с тремя кнопками рядом с ней, которые надо набегу активировать, и там у них такой узкий тайминг, что опоздай ты на 1 десятую секунды, то ты уже его провалил.  Сюжет читается на моменте первого запуска игры, где тебе показывают, что это игра содержит сцены выгорания, депрессии и т.п. В голове сразу же появилась мысль: "Хм, здесь будут буллить главную героиню, говоря, что у неё ничего не выйдет, но она в конце сможет превозмочь?" И да, буквально это в сюжете и происходит. Нет каких-то цепляющих моментов или чего-то, из-за чего хочется сопереживать главной героине.  Боёвка - закликивание одной кнопки. У главной героини если комбо из трёх нажатий кнопки атаки. Оно является самым быстрым способом нанесентя урона, плюс доступно со старту. Ты буквально половину игры просто закликиваешь всех мобов одной кнопкой, изредка юзая спец приём кистью, чтобы полечиться. Позже появляется рывок сквозь врага, но он также не дамажит щитовиков в лоб и наносит столько же урона, сколько один удар.  Я не понимаю, что мешало разработчикам добавить банальную модификацию на плюс урон. Буквально все рядовые враги кроме особых умирают от трёх ударов и так на протяжении всей игры. Ты не чувствуешь какой либо прогрессии. На первой локации ты убиваешь соплю комбой из трёх ударов, так и на финальной ты убиваешь врага этой же самой комбой из трёх ударов. Дизайн врагов тоже неочёмный, ты побеждаешь каких-то безликих роботов, какую-то слизь, какие-то кубы. Тебе даже их как-то сюжетно не обоснуют, из-за чего складывается такое ощущение, что они здесь чисто ради галочки. Боссы (кроме финального) как сюжетные, так и дополнительные такие же безликие статисты, как и обычные враги - они запоминаются только своими неприлично затянутыми боссфайтами. После победы над которыми ты думаешь не "вау, какой крутой босс, хочу его пройти снова", а "кто это вообще такой, ну и фиг с ним".  Не помогает этому и музыка. За всю игру не услышал каких-либо цепляющих треков. Спустя пару попыток на боссе или нескольких минут на локации, мне уже хотелось включить что-нибудь своё, ибо скука смертная. На и так затянутых сражениях с какими-то невзрачными боссами тебе включают скучный монотонный ост, от которого хочется спать.  Так как это первая попытка разработчиков в метроидванию, то всё-таки оставлю положительный отзыв.

单看质量，个人游戏整体评分7.5/10分（如果考虑体量和价格比只有7分），打折可以考虑入手，全价除非其他类银都玩的差不多了，不然不推荐  优点 美术方面：非常优秀，可以说是目前最大的卖点了 手感：人物的战斗和跳跳乐手感都挺优秀的，能看出来是好好打磨了的。  缺点 BOSS设计：BOSS设计大多都是偏机制，交互很一般，尤其是马戏团的BOSS，完全可以说没有交互，和打回合制一样严格限制输出窗口，而且机制需要对电波也没有npc对话提示，初见非常折磨，可BOSS本身除开初见机制杀其实没什么难度的，死几次清楚机制背完板了就可以基本无伤，这种纯粹走机制的打完也没什么成就感，这就导致虽说游戏的战斗手感优秀，但实际BOSS体验却比较差。  体量方面：个人一周目7小时，100%收集10小时左右，在类银中算中等偏低，但对于76的价格绝对算小体量了  任务引导：这游戏后期找猫任务的地点在四个不同的大区域，地点提示还相当的谜语，然后另一个电话的任务说电话线断了，我一开始还以为要找电话线接或者去修电话线，鬼知道是用颜料戳刺戳方向啊，打全收集的时候找这2个任务卡了半个小时就找到一次猫，受不了了找攻略才知道猫的位置和线是用颜料戳刺戳的（不过毕竟是非主线任务，算小扣分点吧）   其他的，跳跳乐设计无功无过，剧情老套但也不算差，音乐还行，但是打完全收集了也没什么记忆点，这些不加分也不扣分

매트로배니아를 평소 좋아해서 구매했고 감정이라는 소재와 연관이 되어있는 스토리다 보니 우울한 감성이 느껴지는 게임인데, 이게 기분 나쁜 느낌이 아니라 잔잔하고 전반적으로 고요한 분위기의 느낌입니다.  물감, 붓, 지우개 등을 활용하여 액션을 표현하는게 신선했고 타격감이 좋았으며 유리가 깨지는 소리, 데미지를 입을때의 소리, 물감 소리 등을 디테일하게 묘사하여 효과음을 잘 표현했다는 생각이 들었어요. 그래픽 또한 감성적인 2d라서 제 취향에 맞았습니다. 죽음 시 패널티도 선택지가 있어서 좋았구요.  그러나 많은 분들이 지적했듯, 매트로배니아 특성상 지나왔던 길도 필히 여러번 재방문을 해야 하거나 길이 복잡한 경우들이 많은데 맵 표현이 너무 안좋고 편의성이 떨어져서 피로감이 들고 답답함이 있었습니다. 지도 자체가 큼직한 네모로만 표시가 되어 있거든요. 이러한 단점을 상쇄하고자 지도 스크린샷 기능이 존재하지만, 그럼에도 불편하다는 느낌이 사라지지는 않았어요.  추후 구슬을 이용하여 순간 이동하는 기술이 생기는데, 구슬의 방향성이 반대로 먹히거나 순간 이동되는 자리의 바로 앞에 가시가 있거나 이런식으로, 바로 대시를 쓰더라도 얻어맞는 플랫폼 구간이 있어서 불합리한 느낌도 조금 들었습니다.  플레이 타임은 짧을것 같고, 전반적으로 애매한 게임이라 느꼈지만 나쁘지도 않았기에 호불호가 조금 있을것 같은 게임입니다. 지도는 아마 업데이트 예정인것 같은데, 추후 단점을 장점으로 바꿀 가능성이 있어보여서 일단 추천으로 평가 남기려고 합니다.

상당히 좋은 게임입니다.  메트로배니아를 좋아하거나 입문하고 싶다면 추천드립니다. 난이도가 어려운 편은 아니지만 가끔식 불쾌한 부분이 나옵니다. 그래도 적당히 참을 수 있는 정도고 난이도가 그리 어려운게 아니라서 괜찮았습니다. 연출도 좋았습니다. 다만 플탐이 많이 짧습니다. 공략도 안보고 그냥 가는 대로 했는데도 6시간 안에 클리어가 가능했습니다. 그래도 충분히 좋은 게임이니 추천드립니다

Придбав гру через її візуальну складову, мені таке дуже подобається. Також метроїдванії - один з моїх улюблених жанрів(гра Hollow Knight 1 це найулюбленіша гра взагалі), а присутність української мови - ще більше підсилило бажання купити цю гру майже на релізі. Я не помилився... Починається все напрочуд стандартно: головна героїня немає ніяких вмінь, а лише вміє бігати та стрибати. З прогресом гри гравець отримує добре знайомі речі в метроїдваніях? подвійний стриборк, ривок і т.д.(є і доволі унікальні). Сюжет дуже сподобався. Навіть подача така, яку я обожнюю. Насправді він дуже глибокий та емоційний. Багато проблематики. Чи сподобалась гра? Так, чорт забирай. Я так давно не кайфував. Не хотілось виходити, але змушували відключення світла. Для розуміння: я награв 19 годин, кожна локація мапи була завершена на 100%, пройшов всі найважчі паркур завдання та додаткові завдання. Здобув всі досягнення(окрім як пройти гру за певний час). Це вже ьагато що означає. В цій грі дуже багато що сподобалось, опишу лише дещицю: - боси дуже класні. Дуже унікальні, деякі механіки бачив вперше(я не претендую на знавця метроїдваній, я пройшов близько 10-15) - система позначок на мапі, щоб пізніше повернутись і пройти те що не зміг. Геніально. В грі є механіка створення світлин. Гравець просто робить світлину і прикріплює її до локації. Пізніше дуже просто дізнатись що такм і чому саме гравець пропустив цю секцію, просто відкривши світлину. І ше рах геніально. - прекрасні секретики. На початку відчувалось доволі примітивно, та ще й зважаючи на те що я звік бити кожну стінку в пошуках секрету. Але пізніше стало і справді складно. Подобається. - Дуже колоритні неігрові персонажі, їх діалоги, їх завдання. Виконував з задоволенням. - Мапа. Спершу ідчувалась незручною, оскільки помічає лише в якій кімнаті перебуває персонаж, але пізніше все стало на свої місця і почало справді подобатись. Грішки: - одне із досягнень мусив підглянути. І справді його виконати можна хіба випадково. - Знайшов 2 помилки в формуванні речення. Це і справді дуже мало. Переклад виконаний пречудово. Думаю  ним все гаразд, робили українці(присутні слова як ,,мисткиня,, і тому подібні)  Грою максимально задодолений, важко передати наскільки. Давно мені щось не приносило такого захоплення без можливості відірватись. Рекомендую!

Ma toute première évaluation, Coutumier des Hollow Knight, Ender Lillies ou encore Celeste, je ne peux que constater à quel point Constance est inférieur à tous ces jeux.  Constance est charmant, Constance aborde des thèmes forts oui mais voilà, niveau gameplay c'est pauvre et trop moyennement executé. On roule sur les zones pour ensuite se farcir du die and retry sur certains boss qu'il faut malheureusement parfois apprendre par coeur. Quand un jeu de plateteforme met à l'épreuve votre mémoire plutôt que vos reflexes, c'est qu'il est passé à côté de son sujet.

Прекрасная игра. У нее чудесный геймплей, которая одновременно может дать суровый челендж в виде паркура, боевки и головоломок, так и не может задушнить. Это метроидвания, а значит часто надо возвращаться в предыдущие локации. Вместо того чтобы душно обходить каждый камень, разрабы дают возможность проверить какие комнаты зачистились, а в каких еще спрятано что-то. Единственный косяк - кристаллы со светляками не влияют на степень "зачистки" карты, из-за чего я некоторое время ломал голову над тем, чтобы получить недостающие светляки для 100% прохождения. Тем не менее, игра хоть и маленькая, часов на 10-15, но каждая МИНУТА игры не давала мне соскучать. Пускай локаций всего 4, но каждая из них чем то уникальна, как и боссы. Также в игре очень приятный визуал и саунд-дизайн. У меня в плейлисте даже хранятся некоторые ОСТы.  Сюжет и лор игры запутан для тех, кому не знакомо состояние ГГ, ведь сюжет повествует не о спасении мира или еще о чем то великом. Это простая "сказка" о гг, о ее внутренних переживаний и проблем, как она поборола их.  Однозначно входит в топ моих любимых метроидваний, ставля ей оценку 9 из 10

Constance é um jogo com fortes inspirações em Celeste e Hollow Knight, em cenários lindos com artes tocantes e lágrimas reflexivas, o jogo em si esquece de fazer valer o quesito Ação, o combate se torna um pouco travado, principalmente pela falta de ataques para cima e para baixo, por mais que as habilidades tentem compensar conforme o jogo progride, você ainda sente falta de velocidade durante a ação.  Em contrapartida, se na ação falta desafios, a parte de plataforma e puzzles se sobressai, a exploração para 100% das salas é satisfatória e te faz pensar além em como resolver, apesar da falta de itens importantes. A falta de upgrades de ferramentas é trocada pelas melhorias das Inspirações. Num geral, o jogo é lindo e uma plataformer envolvente, divertido e desafiador, mas como jogo de ação e metroidvania talvez seja menos do que poderia ter sido, mapa com ótima qualidade para se localizar e pistas de puzzles, mas com fator de monstros e bosses fracos, trilha sonora ótima, apesar de que podia aparecer mais e é claro uma história muito necessária para pessoas que se sentem sobrecarregadas com seus próprios pensamentos e aflições.

very fun and creative game. Great reinterpretation of mechanics as metaphors and gorgeous level world design. Did feel rather short though, like there was a second act that the player missed. I could've(and will) gotten more time exploring the different areas for secrets I may have missed, but I don't think that would've fixed the pacing so much as pushed it back.
